companyInfo/companyDescription:
Die Rhenus Gruppe ist einer der führenden, weltweit operierenden Logistikdienstleister. Entlang der gesamten Supply Chain bieten wir maßgeschneiderte Lösungen für Unternehmen aus den unterschiedlichsten Branchen. Von multimodalen Transporten bis zur perfekten Lagerung, vom reibungslosen Verzollen bis zu innovativen Mehrwertdiensten: Wir sind immer am Puls der Zeit und an der Seite unserer Kunden.
Leitbild: Wir wissen schon heute, was morgen gefragt ist. Für jeden Kunden die passende Lösung finden? Für uns Anspruch und Selbstverständlichkeit zugleich. Was uns als Arbeitgeber besonders macht: Wir stehen mit der gleichen Leidenschaft für unsere Mitarbeitenden ein – und versorgen sie mit allem, was sie für ihre Karriere brauchen.

Reference ID JR122572
Company Name Rhenus Assets & Services GmbH & Co. KG
Locations
Duisburg, Hilden, Holzwickede

Job Category Freight Services
Business Unit
Road Freight
Contact Person Oceane Brachais
+33 (6) 70663647
Job Description
What you can expect

As part of our central Direct Load and Carrier Management team, you will play a key role in the development of our European carrier management network, by executing harmonized carrier sourcing and driving digital initiatives and optimizing processes and workflows. Key responsibilities include:

Support domestic and international branches with executing harmonized annual tenders for regular freight & linehauls, including intensive preparation and follow-up.
Monitor, analyse, and improve data quality to support accurate planning and reporting.
Expand European activities around Carrier Relationship Management and independently manage major carrier accounts.
Identify business needs and translate them into practical process or IT-driven solutions and foster rollout in the carrier community.
Support implementation of group wide state-of-the-art IT-solutions to digitize the business.
Define, track, and analyse product-related KPIs to support continuous improvement initiatives.
What you bring
Education in freight forwarding and logistics services.
Several years of experience in operational roles, such as truck dispatching and freight purchasing ideally on a European level.
Experience in project coordination, carrier management, or software implementation is considered an advantage.
Analytical mindset with excellent attention to detail and data quality.
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills.
Comfortable working in an international and cross-functional environment.
Fluent in German & English; and other foreign languages are an asset.
Willingness to travel.

Location: Germany (preferred Rhein Ruhr area, Düsseldorf, Dortmund, Duisburg etc.) – hybrid model

Business Unit
Road Freight

Logistics needs intelligent management so that goods are in the right place at the right time -our transport solutions guarantee exactly that. Rhenus Road Freight's product portfolio ranges from groupage freight, partial and full loads to hazardous goods, and special transports. Together with accompanying services such as insurance or customs, this results in individual and industry-specific full-service packages. We are pragmatic and uncomplicated. At the same time, our permanent course for growth opens up long-term perspectives for all our employees, whether they are specialists or career changers.

Welcome to Rhenus

Where our journey of growth connects with yours.

We are a global powerhouse on a mission to be pioneers in everything we do. United, we make an impact and dare to redefine what's possible. Ready to unlock untapped potential. We are on the fast track. At Rhenus, we thrive on collaboration. Every voice is heard, and every idea is valued. We stand as one global team. Where everyone contributes to our success. Together, we shape the future of logistics and beyond. Empowered by you.

Recruitment Process
Online application

Your documents will reach us quickly and directly via our online application form; depending on the position, a cover letter may be required. In any case, we are eager to see your CV and references – for us, the best way to make a good first impression.

Application screening

Our HR team and the responsible department will take a close look at your application as soon as possible. We will do our best to give you feedback within two weeks. With your agreement, we would also be happy to forward the documents to other business units if the selected position does not work out.

Selection Process

Depending on the position, there are various ways of finding out if we are a good match for each other. In most cases, we rely on a personal interview, on a eye level of course. Online tests, assessment centres or work on a trial basis may also be possible. In the end, the best decision is taken for both sides.

Onboarding

With your commitment, you are a part of the Rhenus Group right away – we are already eager to shape the logistics of the future together with you. To ensure that your start is a complete success, we will familiarise you with everything you need to know about Rhenus and your assignment with us. Your experienced colleagues will guide you every step of the way, and together you will easily master each of your new tasks.

companyInfo/companyDescription:
Mit über 100.000 engagierten Arbeitnehmern in mehr als 90 Ländern entwickeln wir die Energiesysteme der Zukunft und stellen den wachsenden Energiebedarf der Menschen weltweit verlässlich und nachhaltig sicher.

Durch die in unseren Forschungsabteilungen und Fabriken entwickelten Technologien treiben wir die Energiewende voran und schaffen die Grundlage für ein Sechstel der weltweiten Energieerzeugung. Unser weltweites Team arbeitet daran, nachhaltige, zuverlässige und erschwingliche Energie Wirklichkeit werden zu lassen, indem es die Grenzen des Möglichen immer weiter verschiebt.

Seit über 150 Jahren stehen wir für Innovation und suchen nach Menschen, die uns dabei helfen, die Dekarbonisierung, neue Technologien und die Energiewende schwerpunktmäßig voranzutreiben. Möglichkeiten entdecken, um bei Siemens Energy einen Beitrag zu leisten.
description:
Mode of Employment: Full-time or part-time / Permanent

A Snapshot of Your Day

As a Lead Digitalization Expert in Digital Operations (f/m/d), you act as the hands‑on digital partner for Tender and Project Management within FACTS and Storage projects. You define and drive the digital strategy across the tender‑to‑project lifecycle, simplifying workflows, improving data consistency, and increasing transparency. Working closely with business teams and IT, you transform complex tool landscapes and manual processes into scalable, embedded digital solutions that deliver measurable impact for a fast‑growing, execution‑focused business.

How You’ll Make an Impact
Leadership of digitalization for tender and project management processes in the FACTS and Storage environment, including preparation, handover, execution transparency, and reporting
Implementation of digital solutions and process improvements in alignment with TPS standards, Digital Operations guardrails, and the end‑to‑end project operating model
Enablement of collaboration and adoption through facilitation of targeted workshops with Tender Management, Project Management, and Project Controls
Strengthening of data foundations and data literacy through improved consistency, structure, and reuse of tender and project data across phases and systems
Identification and prioritization of high‑value digital opportunities to reduce manual effort, improve handover quality, and increase project predictability
Cross‑functional collaboration with Digital Operations, IT, Engineering, Procurement, Logistics, and Site Management to ensure integrated and future‑proof digital workflows across the project lifecycle
What You Bring
Degree in Engineering, Project Management, Business Administration, Information Systems, or a comparable field
Several years of relevant work experience in tendering, project management, or digitalization within repetitive project environments or FACTS/Storage contexts
Strong understanding of end‑to‑end tender‑to‑project workflows and the practical requirements of Project Managers and Tender Leads
Experience with project management tools, data structures, and digital solutions supporting project execution as an advantage
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills for effective cross‑functional collaboration
Structured, pragmatic, and hands‑on working style with a strong focus on operational impact
About the Team

Our Grid Technology division enables a reliable, sustainable, and digital grid. The power grid is the backbone of the energy transition. Siemens Energy offers a leading portfolio and solutions in HVDC transmission, grid stabilization and storage, high voltage switchgears and transformers, and digital grid technology.

Who is Siemens Energy?

At Siemens Energy, we are more than just an energy technology company. With ~100.000 dedicated employees in more than 90 countries, we develop the energy systems of the future, ensuring that the growing energy demand of the global community is met reliably and sustainably. The technologies created in our research departments and factories drive the energy transition and provide the base for one sixth of the world's electricity generation.

Our global team is committed to making sustainable, reliable, and affordable energy a reality by pushing the boundaries of what is possible. We uphold a 150-year legacy of innovation that encourages our search for people who will support our focus on decarbonization, new technologies, and energy transformation.

Find out how you can make a difference at Siemens Energy: https://www.siemens-energy.com/employeevideo

Our Commitment to Diversity

Lucky for us, we are not all the same. Through diversity we generate power. We run on inclusion and our combined creative energy is fueled by over 130 nationalities. Siemens Energy celebrates character – no matter what ethnic background, gender, age, religion, identity, or disability. We energize society, all of society, and we do not discriminate based on our differences.

Rewards/Benefits
In addition to an attractive remuneration package in line with the market, you can expect an attractive employer-financed company pension scheme
We also offer the opportunity to become a Siemens Energy shareholder
We offer our employees the opportunity to work flexibly and remotely, and our inspiring offices provide space for collaboration and creativity
The professional and personal development of our employees is very important to us. We provide them with the opportunities to learn and develop in a self-determined way, various attractive programmes and learning materials are available for this purpose
In relation to the "compatibility of family and work", we have a wide range of offers, e.g. flexible working time models, childcare places at many locations, the possibility of trial part-time work or even a sabbatical
We value equal opportunities and welcome applications from people with disabilities.

https://jobs.siemens-energy.com/jobs

companyInfo/companyDescription:
At Siemens Healthineers USA, our purpose is to enable healthcare providers to increase value by empowering them on their journey towards expanding precision medicine, transforming care delivery, and improving patient experience, all enabled by digitalizing healthcare.

An estimated five million patients globally everyday benefit from our innovative technologies and services in the areas of diagnostic and therapeutic imaging, laboratory diagnostics and molecular medicine, as well as digital health and enterprise services.

We are a leading medical technology company with over 120 years of experience and 18,500 patents globally. With about 50,000 dedicated colleagues in over 70 countries, we will continue to innovate and shape the future of healthcare.
description:
Do you want to help create the future of healthcare? Our name, Siemens Healthineers, was selected to honor our people who dedicate their energy and passion to this cause. It reflects their pioneering spirit combined with our long history of engineering in the ever-evolving healthcare industry.

We offer you a flexible and dynamic environment with opportunities to go beyond your comfort zone in order to grow personally and professionally. Sounds interesting?

Then come and join our global team as Global Lead for Climate Strategy & Governance (f/m/d), for defining, governing, and driving the company’s climate strategy and climate action agenda. The role owns the end-to-end framework for climate change mitigation and adaptation, including net zero strategy, target setting, roadmap development, implementation oversight, and achievement, as well as climate-related risk, resilience, and governance.

Operating from within the corporate sustainability function, the role ensures consistent, and credible implementation across all business areas, regions, and functions, in alignment with external standards, regulatory requirements, and internal governance expectations.

Choose the best place for your work – Within the scope of this position, it is possible, in consultation with your manager, to work mobile (within Germany) up to an average volume of 60% of the respective working hours.

Even more flexibility? Mobile working from abroad is possible for up to 30 days a year under certain conditions and in selected countries.

Your tasks and responsibilities:

You own and continuously develop the company’s global climate strategy and net‑zero framework, including targets, roadmaps, milestones, and performance tracking, aligned with recognized external standards.
You define and govern the climate action program across Scopes 1–3, translating strategy into clear implementation requirements, linking climate targets to business KPIs, and ensuring progress tracking and corrective actions.
You drive alignment between climate action, circularity, and sustainable design by collaborating with R&D, Procurement, Quality, and business teams, and by advancing climate‑related methodologies, data, and tools (e.g. product carbon footprints).
You lead the company’s approach to climate risk, adaptation, and resilience, including risk assessments, scenario analysis, and integration into enterprise risk management and strategic planning.
You establish robust climate governance, ensuring compliance with regulatory and reporting requirements (e.g. CSRD/ESRS), audit‑ready climate data and disclosures, and proactive monitoring of regulatory and standards developments.
You translate climate strategy into customer and stakeholder value, supporting customer requirements, tenders, and external climate‑related inquiries.
You represent the company externally on climate topics in industry initiatives and engagements with investors, customers, and media.
You build, lead, and develop a high‑performing climate team and foster strong cross‑functional collaboration.

Your qualifications and experience:

You have a university degree in Sustainability, Environmental Science, Engineering, Business, Public Policy, or a related field; additional qualifications in climate, energy, or ESG are an advantage.
You have many years of professional experience leading climate strategy and climate action in a large, mature organization, with demonstrated accountability for net zero or science-based targets, roadmaps, and delivery.
You have deep technical expertise in climate change mitigation, including Greenhouse Gas accounting (Scope 1, 2, and 3), SBTi and Net Zero standards, and the translation of targets into credible implementation pathways.
You have a strong understanding of climate-related risk and resilience, including governance and regulation expectations (e.g. CSRD/ESRS, TCFD-aligned disclosures, jurisdiction-specific climate regulation), with experience engaging external and internal auditors.
You have a very good understanding of sustainable design, lifecycle thinking, and climate‑related data and tool landscapes, including the challenges of scaling robust, decision‑relevant carbon data (e.g. product carbon footprints).
You have a proven track record of implementation in a matrixed environment, working closely with and across functions such as Procurement, Real Estate, EHS, Finance, Risk, and R&D to translate strategy into action.
You are an experienced people and program leader, able to build and lead high-performing teams, manage complex cross-functional initiatives, and deliver consistent results. Prior people management experience is a plus.

Your attributes and skills:

You have fluent English language skills (verbal & written)
You are a strategic thinker with a hands-on mindset, comfortable navigating ambiguity, balancing ambition with feasibility, and connecting climate strategy with operational and business realities.
You have excellent communication and influencing skills, with the ability to align diverse stakeholders, prepare high‑quality materials for senior management, and engage confidently with customers, investors, media, and other external stakeholders.
You have the ability to build and leverage a strong internal and external network across functions, regions, and key stakeholders to drive alignment, collaboration, and impact.

Our global team:

Siemens Healthineers is a leading global medical technology company. 74,000 dedicated colleagues in over 70 countries are driven to shape the future of healthcare. An estimated 5 million patients across the globe benefit every day from our innovative technologies and services in the areas of diagnostic and therapeutic imaging, laboratory diagnostics and molecular medicine, as well as digital health and enterprise services.

Our culture:

Our culture embraces different perspectives, open debate, and the will to challenge convention. Change is a constant aspect of our work. We aspire to lead the change in our industry rather than just react to it. That’s why we invite you to take on new challenges, test your ideas, and celebrate success. Check our Careers Site at https://www.siemens-healthineers.com/de/careers

As an equal opportunity employer, we welcome applications from individuals with disabilities.#myabilityshs

Wish to find out more before applying? Contact us: recruitinggersupport.func@siemens-healthineers.com, if you wish to discuss any initial questions with our recruitment team. The contact person handling this job ad is Klaus Kouril.

We care about your data privacy and take compliance with GDPR as well as other data protection legislation seriously. For this reason, we ask you not to send us your CV or resume by email. We ask instead that you create a profile in our talent community where you can upload your CV. Setting up a profile lets us know you are interested in career opportunities with us and makes it easy for us to send you an alert when relevant positions become open. Click here to get started.

To all recruitment agencies:

Siemens Healthineers' recruitment is internally managed, with external support permitted only when a qualified supplier has established a formal contract with us. Unsolicited candidate submissions and referrals, absent a current supplier contract, do not establish consent and are ineligible for fees. We delete and destroy unsolicited information, thus, would recommend you refrain from any such practices. Your adherence to our policies is appreciated.

Siemens Healthineers Germany was awarded the Great Place to Work® certificate.

We care about your data privacy and take compliance with GDPR as well as other data protection legislation seriously. For this reason, we ask you not to send us your CV or resume by email. We ask instead that you create a profile in our talent community where you can upload your CV. Setting up a profile lets us know you are interested in career opportunities with us and makes it easy for us to send you an alert when relevant positions become open. Click here to get started.


To all recruitment agencies:

Siemens Healthineers' recruitment is internally managed, with external support permitted only when a qualified supplier has established a formal contract with us. We delete and destroy unsolicited information, thus, would recommend you refrain from any such practices. Your adherence to our policies is appreciated.

companyInfo/companyDescription:
Mit über 100.000 engagierten Arbeitnehmern in mehr als 90 Ländern entwickeln wir die Energiesysteme der Zukunft und stellen den wachsenden Energiebedarf der Menschen weltweit verlässlich und nachhaltig sicher.

Durch die in unseren Forschungsabteilungen und Fabriken entwickelten Technologien treiben wir die Energiewende voran und schaffen die Grundlage für ein Sechstel der weltweiten Energieerzeugung. Unser weltweites Team arbeitet daran, nachhaltige, zuverlässige und erschwingliche Energie Wirklichkeit werden zu lassen, indem es die Grenzen des Möglichen immer weiter verschiebt.

Seit über 150 Jahren stehen wir für Innovation und suchen nach Menschen, die uns dabei helfen, die Dekarbonisierung, neue Technologien und die Energiewende schwerpunktmäßig voranzutreiben. Möglichkeiten entdecken, um bei Siemens Energy einen Beitrag zu leisten.
description:
A Snapshot of Your Day

As a Logistics Project Manager (m/f/d), you will take ownership of complex international transport solutions for large-scale energy projects and ensure that critical components reach their destination safely, efficiently, and on time. From heavy goods and oversized transports to highly customized logistics concepts, you will play a key role in enabling the global energy transition.

Working in an international project environment, you will coordinate logistics partners, customers, and internal stakeholders across multiple regions while managing budgets, risks, schedules, and transport strategies. You will contribute your expertise during both the tender and execution phases, developing innovative logistics concepts tailored to customer and country-specific requirements.

How You’ll Make an Impact
Management and coordination of heavy goods, ODC, and general cargo shipments, including budget responsibility, cost calculation, savings identification, and risk minimization
Steering and coordination of international logistics teams to ensure smooth transport chains during project execution
Support of project tender phases through development of complex transport concepts for international technical large-scale projects in line with customer and project requirements
Conducting risk assessments and evaluations as well as reviewing customer specifications and EPC contracts
Management of international logistics and service providers while ensuring compliance with transport contracts, costs, deadlines, and contractual requirements
Coordination of transport concepts with international internal and external stakeholders and project partners
What You Bring
Completed commercial or technical training, ideally as a Freight Forwarding and Logistics Services Clerk with several years experience in project logistics, ideally within international projects and complex logistics solutions or university degree in Business Administration or Logistics Management with first relevant experience
Experience working in international cross-functional teams with strong coordination skills and solid knowledge of logistics and contract law
Knowledge of risk, claim, and opportunity management as well as PM@Siemens-related process understanding is desirable
Fluent English and ideally good German skills together with good MS Office and ideally SAP knowledge
Strong analytical, organizational, communication, and teamwork skills combined with customer orientation, assertiveness, and a proactive mindset for process improvement and innovation

About the Team

Our Grid Technology division enables a reliable, sustainable, and digital grid. The power grid is the backbone of the energy transition. Siemens Energy offers a leading portfolio and solutions in HVDC transmission, grid stabilization and storage, high voltage switchgears and transformers, and digital grid technology.

Who is Siemens Energy?

At Siemens Energy, we are more than just an energy technology company. With ~100.000 dedicated employees in more than 90 countries, we develop the energy systems of the future, ensuring that the growing energy demand of the global community is met reliably and sustainably. The technologies created in our research departments and factories drive the energy transition and provide the base for one sixth of the world's electricity generation.

Our global team is committed to making sustainable, reliable, and affordable energy a reality by pushing the boundaries of what is possible. We uphold a 150-year legacy of innovation that encourages our search for people who will support our focus on decarbonization, new technologies, and energy transformation.

Find out how you can make a difference at Siemens Energy: https://www.siemens-energy.com/employeevideo

Our Commitment to Diversity

Lucky for us, we are not all the same. Through diversity we generate power. We run on inclusion and our combined creative energy is fueled by over 130 nationalities. Siemens Energy celebrates character – no matter what ethnic background, gender, age, religion, identity, or disability. We energize society, all of society, and we do not discriminate based on our differences.

Rewards/Benefits
In addition to an attractive remuneration package in line with the market, you can expect an attractive employer-financed company pension scheme
We also offer the opportunity to become a Siemens Energy shareholder
We offer our employees the opportunity to work flexibly and remotely, and our inspiring offices provide space for collaboration and creativity
The professional and personal development of our employees is very important to us. We provide them with the opportunities to learn and develop in a self-determined way, various attractive programmes and learning materials are available for this purpose
In relation to the "compatibility of family and work", we have a wide range of offers, e.g. flexible working time models, childcare places at many locations, the possibility of trial part-time work or even a sabbatical
We value equal opportunities and welcome applications from people with disabilities.

https://jobs.siemens-energy.com/jobs

Reference ID
JR119172
Company Name
Rhenus Air & Ocean Management GmbH & Co.KG
Locations
Frankfurt, Schiphol

Job Category
Sales & Key Account Management
Business Unit

Air & Ocean
Contact Person
Robert Dembinski
+49 (175) 6804895
JOB DESCRIPTION
WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT

1. Strategy, Governance & Performance

Be part of setting the global LSH vertical strategy and execution in your region for Air & Ocean, including annual planning for revenue, volume, and gross profit in line with agreed budgets.
Drive vertical growth initiatives, including account penetration, capability development, and business improvement programs aligned with customer expectations.
Act as the link between strategy and execution, translating strategic objectives into concrete initiatives, projects, and governance structures.
Lead, coach, and develop Key Account Management and Business Development resources within the LSH vertical.

2. Business Development & Key Accounts

Identify, develop, and manage high-potential and strategic global LSH customers, maintaining a robust sales pipeline.
Personally engage and drive in strategic sales opportunities, customer negotiations, and executive-level meetings where required.
Provide commercial and analytical input during tender and RFQ/RFP processes, including historic volume, rate, and profitability analysis.
Represent the LSH vertical in customer meetings, industry events, and relevant external forums.
Act as single point of escalation for key LSH customers, ensuring service quality, customer satisfaction, and rapid issue resolution.

3. Quality & Compliance

Ensure quality governance and regulatory compliance across the LSH vertical within Air & Ocean within your region.
Ensure adherence to relevant LSH industry standards and customer requirements (e.g. GDP, GxP, temperature-controlled logistics, validation, audit readiness).
Translate regulatory and customer requirements into standardized operational frameworks, controls, and KPIs across the global network.
Work closely with Quality, Operations, Product, and Sales to ensure customer solutions, contracts, and service commitments are fully compliant and risk-assessed.
Act as senior escalation point for quality deviations, audits, and compliance-related customer issues, ensuring structured root-cause analysis and corrective actions.
Drive continuous improvement initiatives to enhance compliance maturity, service quality, and risk management across LSH supply chains.

4. Internal Alignment & Network Engagement

Work closely with Global, Regional, and Country Sales leadership to ensure consistent vertical strategy execution across regions.
Build strong cross-functional collaboration with Product, Operations, Pricing, Tender Management, and other Business Units.
Drive internal awareness of LSH capabilities through structured communication, training, and best-practice sharing.
Ensure alignment between customer commitments and operational capabilities across the global network.
WHAT YOU BRING

Experience & Capabilities

Proven senior experience in freight forwarding or logistics, with a strong focus on Life Sciences & Healthcare and global customer management.
Demonstrated ability to lead strategic growth initiatives, manage senior stakeholders, and develop high-performing commercial teams.
Strong understanding of value creation in logistics, including cost-to-serve, productivity, total cost of ownership, and inventory considerations.
Hands-on experience with tender management, solution design, and complex deal execution.
Excellent communication, negotiation, and stakeholder management skills in an international environment.
Analytical, structured, and execution-oriented, with the ability to operate independently in a complex matrix organization.
Fluent in English; additional languages are an advantage.
BUSINESS UNIT
AIR & OCEAN

Regardless of whether it is a long-distance shipment or a particularly time-critical shipment, we at Rhenus make everything possible for our customers and partners to get the goods to their destination safely and on time. Our particular strength lies in our global network, which is constantly being maintained and expanded. As part of Rhenus Air & Ocean you can, therefore, expect to find a whole host of exciting tasks all over the world, a wide range of opportunities for personal development, and the chance to take on responsibility in an international working environment.

WELCOME TO RHENUS

Where our journey of growth connects with yours.

We are a global powerhouse on a mission to be pioneers in everything we do. United, we make an impact and dare to redefine what's possible. Ready to unlock untapped potential. We are on the fast track. At Rhenus, we thrive on collaboration. Every voice is heard, and every idea is valued. We stand as one global team. Where everyone contributes to our success. Together, we shape the future of logistics and beyond. Empowered by you.

RECRUITMENT PROCESS
ONLINE APPLICATION

Your documents will reach us quickly and directly via our online application form; depending on the position, a cover letter may be required. In any case, we are eager to see your CV and references – for us, the best way to make a good first impression.

APPLICATION SCREENING

Our HR team and the responsible department will take a close look at your application as soon as possible. We will do our best to give you feedback within two weeks. With your agreement, we would also be happy to forward the documents to other business units if the selected position does not work out.

SELECTION PROCESS

Depending on the position, there are various ways of finding out if we are a good match for each other. In most cases, we rely on a personal interview, on a eye level of course. Online tests, assessment centres or work on a trial basis may also be possible. In the end, the best decision is taken for both sides.

ONBOARDING

With your commitment, you are a part of the Rhenus Group right away – we are already eager to shape the logistics of the future together with you. To ensure that your start is a complete success, we will familiarise you with everything you need to know about Rhenus and your assignment with us. Your experienced colleagues will guide you every step of the way, and together you will easily master each of your new tasks.

Job Description

What you can expect

Management of End-to-End Customer Implementations:

  • Manage and execute global implementation projects for our top accounts from scope to delivery, coordinating stakeholders and timelines.
  • Lead the customer onboarding process, collaborating with customers and internal teams to ensure smooth integration into operational processes.
  • Create and maintain essential documentation including project plans, Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), Internal Operating Procedures (IOPs), process flowcharts, and performance reports.
  • Conduct training and rollout of procedures to internal as well as external stakeholders.

Customer Requirement Analysis and Service Alignment:

  • Collaborate with customers and internal teams (e.g., Key Account Managers, Tender Management, Product) to gather and assess requirements.
  • Perform feasibility checks and translate customer needs into operational processes with Operational Excellence and OKAMs.
  • Ensure effective use of solutions such as integration tools, tracking, and performance dashboards.
  • Stay current on product features and share updates with sales and account teams.

Stakeholder Engagement and Communication:

  • Act as the first point of contact for customers and internal stakeholders during the onboarding process.
  • Facilitate the onboarding of OKAMs during the implementation process and maintain close alignment to ensure a smooth transition and sustainable embedding of the customer into the organization post-implementation.

Reporting and Data Analytics:

  • Define customer reporting needs and work with the BI team to deliver dashboards and data tools.
  • Develop hybrid reporting in Excel if needed and train OKAMs on reporting frameworks.

Process Improvement and Standardization

  • Proactively identify and recommend enhancements to streamline customer implementation processes, contributing to standardization efforts and continuous process optimization initiatives.
  • Actively contribute to the ongoing development, documentation, and enhancement of the global implementation framework and standardization initiatives.

Risk Management and Issue Resolution

  • Proactively identify and mitigate risks throughout the onboarding process.
  • Lead resolution of implementation issues in collaboration with internal and external stakeholders.
    • Capture and
  • lessons learned to improve future implementations.

What you bring

  • Degree in Business Administration, Logistics, Supply Chain Management, or related field.
  • 3-5 years' experience in customer implementation, project management, or operational excellence within Supply Chain, Freight Forwarding, or Control Tower environments.
  • Strong operational and supply chain process knowledge, with hands-on TMS experience.
  • Foundational understanding of IT system integrations and software solutions.
  • Self-driven, ambitious, and team-oriented with a problem-solving mindset and attention to detail.
  • Strong project management, analytical, and conceptual skills; confident in decision-making and risk balancing.
  • Comfortable in fast-paced, high-accountability settings with a strong sense of ownership and urgency.
  • Advanced MS Office skills (Excel); BI tool experience preferred.
  • Excellent interpersonal and stakeholder communication skills (written, verbal, and presentation).
  • Fluent in English & German

Business Unit

Air & Ocean

Regardless of whether it is a long-distance shipment or a particularly time-critical shipment, we at Rhenus make everything possible for our customers and partners to get the goods to their destination safely and on time. Our particular strength lies in our global network, which is constantly being maintained and expanded. With your agreement, we would also be happy to forward the documents to other business units if the selected position does not work out.

companyInfo/companyDescription:
Die Universität Rostock wurde im Jahre 1419 gegründet und ist damit eine der ältesten Hochschulen Deutschlands sowie die älteste Universität im Ostseeraum.



Im Wintersemester 2021/22 waren 12.665 Studierende immatrikuliert, darunter auch viele Erasmus- und weitere Auslandsstudenten. 2018 lehrten in Rostock 275 Professoren. Insgesamt werden von der Universität etwas mehr als 2.900 Mitarbeiter (darunter ca. 850 in der Universitätsmedizin) direkt beschäftigt. Die Mehrheit der über 100 Studiengänge in zehn Fakultäten wird mit Master- und Bachelorabschlüssen angeboten. Die Universität Rostock gilt auf Grund ihres umfangreichen Studienangebots als Volluniversität. In einem 2018 veröffentlichten Ranking von Times Higher Education wurde die Hochschule als die schönste Universität Deutschlands und die viertschönste Europas gelistet.



Hauptgebäude der Universität Rostock

Mehrere Nobelpreisträger haben in Rostock studiert oder geforscht, darunter Albrecht Kossel, Karl von Frisch und Otto Stern. Im Umfeld der Universität haben sich durch Institute, Gründungen und Unternehmensansiedlungen Cluster der Wirtschaft und Forschung in Bereichen der Spitzentechnologie gebildet. Vor allem in den Sektoren Biotechnologie, Medizin und Lebenswissenschaften (zum Beispiel BioCon Valley und DZNE), Nanotechnologie, in der Lichtforschung und Photonik, Agrar- und Umweltwissenschaften, in der maritimen Forschung (IOW) sowie in der Erforschung von Bereichen der Datenverarbeitung und Informationsgesellschaft (Fraunhofer-Institut für Graphische Datenverarbeitung) haben sich bedeutende Vertreter angesiedelt. Auch die demografische Forschung im Umfeld der Rostocker Universität, die über mehrere Sonderforschungsbereiche verfügt, ist von Weltrang, vor allem mit dem zuständigen Max-Planck-Institut.
description:
The University of Rostock offers a diverse, varied and challenging position in a tradition-conscious, yet innovative, modern and family-friendly university in a lively city by the sea.

At the Faculty of Agriculture, Civil Engineering and Environment, Chair of Soil Sciences, subject to allocation of funds, we are filling the following position as of 01.07.2026 on a temporary basis for the duration of the project "WETSCAPES 2.0" ending on 31.12.2028:

Research Assistant (m/f/d) - Soil Sciences

Start date as of 01.07.2026

Working hours full-time with 40 hours

Remuneration pay group 13 TV-L

Location Rostock

Tender number P 67/2026

Limitation limited until 31.12.2028

Application time 2026-06-17

Please do not hesitate to contact us for further information:

HR department:
Anja Kunert
Phone number: 0381/498-1296
E-mail:

Department:
Prof. Dr. Sara Bauke
Phone number: 0381/498-3120
E-mail:

We are seeking a postdoctoral researcher with a strong interest in biogeochemical processes in peat soils. Our goal is to characterize phosphorus and sulfur dynamics in rewetted percolation and coastal fens and, through microcosm studies, to investigate nutrient release from old peat layers under carbon priming as well as nutrient sequestration in new peat layers. To this end, we will combine analyses of enzyme activity and stable isotope tracers—as indicators of phosphorus and sulfur dynamics—with hydrophysical data to estimate nutrient fluxes within peat profiles and rewetted peatland landscapes.

THESE ARE YOUR TASKS:
research on nutrient storage and release in rewetted peat soils as part of the WETSCAPES 2.0 consortium, with the aim of advancing one’s academic qualifications (preparing a scientific publication, preparing for a postdoctoral qualification)
collecting soil samples in field experiments
setting up and maintaining microcosm experiments
analyzing concentrations of nutrients and dissolved organic matter, as well as the release potential through enzymatic activity
developing methods for analyzing oxygen stable isotopes in soil and water samples as tracers for nutrient cycling in peatlands
analyzing data and performing isotope modeling
presenting results at international conferences and publishing in relevant peer-reviewed journals
collaborative experimental work with other doctoral and postdoctoral researchers within the framework of WETSCAPES 2.0
THIS MAKES YOU A GOOD FIT:
a completed university degree (state examination, diploma, master’s degree, or equivalent) in soil science, biogeochemistry, biogeosciences, environmental sciences, or a related field, with at least a good grade
a completed doctoral degree in the above-mentioned fields
excellent knowledge of biogeochemical nutrient cycles, preferably in peatland ecosystems
experience in the application of stable isotope analysis as a tracer for nutrient cycling in soil
ability to design and conduct laboratory and field experiments, including sampling campaigns
very good self-management skills as well as the ability to document and communicate results
very good written and spoken English skills, basic knowledge of the German language or the willingness to acquire it quickly
minimum physical fitness required for laboratory work and for conducting field experiments
strong willingness to collaborate in an international and interdisciplinary team
a Class B driver’s license is desirable
WE AS AN EMPLOYER:

Equal opportunities are important to us. We welcome applications from suitable severely disabled people or people from traditionally underrepresented groups. We aim to increase the proportion of women in research and teaching and therefore encourage suitably qualified women to apply. We welcome applications from people of other nationalities or with a migration background.

WE OFFER YOU:
FURTHER INFORMATION:

We will determine the experience level individually, taking into account your previous professional experience.

If you would like to work part-time in this position, this is possible subject to the requirements of the position.

The temporal limitation of the employment relationship is based on § 2 (1) Wissenschaftszeitvertragsgesetz.

Please note that an (extended) certificate of good conduct must be submitted upon recruitment.

We look forward to receiving your application (cover letter, CV, degree certificate stating your final grade) by 17.06.2026 at the latest. We can only consider applications received via our homepage. Please send us your documents via the ‘Online application’ button at the end of a job offer. Unfortunately, we cannot accept e-mail applications.

Incomplete application documents may not be considered in the further course of the selection process.

Unfortunately, we can not cover application and travel costs.

Become part of the team

The Collaborative Research Center “WETSCAPES 2.0,” funded by the German Research Foundation (DFG), brings together the expertise of the University of Greifswald, the University of Rostock, the IGB Berlin, the GFZ Potsdam, Ludwig Maximilian University of Munich, the MPI for Biogeochemistry in Jena, and Humboldt University of Berlin. The focus of WETSCAPES 2.0 is on the functional understanding of rewetted fens, including coastal fens, in Mecklenburg-Western Pomerania. The rewetting of formerly drained and often agriculturally used lowland fens leads to the development of novel ecosystems that differ from natural bogs and have remained largely unexplored to date. For more information on the overarching research questions, the individual topics, and the contact persons, please visit https://wetscapes2.uni-greifswald.de

We look forward to receiving your application!

Contact

Universität Rostock
18051 Rostock
Tel.: +49 381 498 - 0

Sitze des Rektorats:
Universitätsplatz 1
18055 Rostock

The University of Rostock offers a diverse, varied and challenging position in a tradition-conscious, yet innovative, modern and family-friendly university in a lively city by the sea.

At the Faculty of Computer Science and Electrical Engineering, Institute for Visual and Analytic Computing, subject to allocation of funds, we are filling the following two positions at the earliest possible date on a temporary basis for the duration of the project NEXCELL ending on 31.12.2028:

Research Assistant (m/f/d) - AI

Start date at the earliest possible date

Working hours full-time with 40 hours

Remuneration pay group 13 TV-L

Location Rostock

Tender number P 64/2026

Limitation limited until 31.12.2028

Application time 2026-06-08

Please do not hesitate to contact us for further information:

HR department:
Pia-Lucy Dahl
Phone number: 0381/498-1291
E-mail:

Department:
Prof. Dr.-Ing. Thomas Kirste
Phone number: 0381/498-7510
E-mail:

NEXCELL is a major multi-million-€ collaborative research initiative that brings together leading industrial and academic partners to create a groundbreaking point-of-care platform for next-generation cell and gene therapies. The vision is to enable personalized cancer treatments to be manufactured directly at the clinical site – making life-saving therapies more accessible and scalable. Within this project, the University of Rostock serves as the AI technology provider, developing a probabilistic digital twin of the NEXCELL bioreactor system. This digital twin will combine Bayesian AI, deep learning, symbolic reasoning, and hybrid modeling techniques to intelligently monitor and predict both technical and biological processes. By addressing challenges such as uncertainty quantification, multimodal sensor fusion, anomaly detection, and robust state estimation, our research will push the frontiers of AI in complex, safety-critical, and data-sparse domains. For motivated AI researchers, NEXCELL offers a unique opportunity to conduct fundamental research at the interface of cutting-edge machine learning and real-world bioprocess applications, with the potential for high-impact publications, open-source contributions, and direct collaboration with a market leader in bioreactor technology.

We invite an outstanding doctoral researcher to join our institute in collaboration with a global industrial leader in bioreactor technology. The successful candidate will contribute to a large-scale, interdisciplinary research project focused on the development and deployment of advanced artificial intelligence methods for system monitoring, state estimation, and decision support in next-generation bioreactor platforms.

The project addresses theoretical and applied challenges at the intersection of machine learning, probabilistic modeling, symbolic AI, single cell analysis, and bioprocess engineering, with strong relevance to both academic research and industrial innovation. The position offers a clearly defined pathway toward doctoral qualification, as well as structured opportunities for career development in academia and industry.

Application Documents and Procedure

Applications must be submitted in complete form and include all required formal documents, in particular:

a curriculum vitae,
certificates and transcripts of academic degrees,
proof of language proficiency.

Applicants must further submit a cover letter in which they explicitly address and document compliance with the six eligibility criteria ("this makes you a good fit").

Incomplete applications or applications not meeting the eligibility criteria may not be considered.

After submission, the application process is multi-staged and will include analytical tasks as part of the selection procedure.

THESE ARE YOUR TASKS:
design, implement, and evaluate deep neural, probabilistic, and hybrid symbolic-learning models for real-time sensor fusion, anomaly detection, and latent state estimation in dynamic bioreactor environments
adapt and extend large language models (LLMs) and symbolic reasoning frameworks to interpret domain-specific process logs, develop predictive diagnostics, and support explainable and trustworthy decision-making pipelines for bioprocess engineers
develop multi-modal bioinformatics pipelines for temporal single-cell analysis based on longitudinal pattern mining, geometric and graph deep learning
investigate reinforcement learning, simulator-based inference, and adaptive control strategies for feedback control and trajectory tracking in bioprocesses characterized by high uncertainty and complex nonlinear dynamics
collaborate closely with industrial partners and domain experts to curate multimodal process data, design and execute validation experiments, perform simulation studies, and iteratively refine models for deployment in real-world bioprocess settings
THIS MAKES YOU A GOOD FIT:
academic qualifications:
completed bachelor's and master’s degree in Computer Science, or a 5-year-diploma in Computer Science (or a comparative course of study with a predominantly computer science curriculum)
the qualifying degree must have been completed with good results (2.0 or better according to the German grading system; corresponding to a CGPA of approximately 85% or higher)
degree must have been awarded by a higher education institution (recognized as H+ according to the German Anabin system)
language proficiency: applicants must meet either of the following language profiles:
proficiency in written and spoken English at level C1 CEFR (e.g. IELTS overall score ≥ 7.0, or TOEFL ≥ 94); or
native-level proficiency in German and demonstrated English language skill
research experience and analytical skillset:
documented experience in artificial intelligence, statistical / probabilistic modeling and/or data analysis is required
demonstrated analytical skillset, ability to solve novel and complex tasks independently and present results coherently
organizational and time-management skills: demonstrated ability to work independently and reliably under time constraints and to meet deadlines
communication skills: proven ability to communicate scientific results effectively, both orally and in written form
interdisciplinary competence: demonstrated ability to work collaboratively in interdisciplinary research environments
WE AS AN EMPLOYER:

Equal opportunities are important to us. We welcome applications from suitable severely disabled people or people from traditionally underrepresented groups. We aim to increase the proportion of women in research and teaching and therefore encourage suitably qualified women to apply. We welcome applications from people of other nationalities or with a migration background.

WE OFFER YOU:
FURTHER INFORMATION:

We will determine the experience level individually, taking into account your previous professional experience.

If you would like to work part-time in this position, this is possible subject to the requirements of the position.

The temporal limitation of the employment relationship is based on § 2 (2) Wissenschaftszeitvertragsgesetz.

We look forward to receiving your application (cover letter, CV, degree certificate stating your final grade) by 08.06.2026 at the latest. We can only consider applications received via our homepage. Please send us your documents via the ‘Online application’ button at the end of a job offer. Unfortunately, we cannot accept e-mail applications.

Incomplete application documents may not be considered in the further course of the selection process.

Unfortunately, we can not cover application and travel costs.

Become part of the team

The Hybrid Methods in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning group is part of the Institute for Visual & Analytic Computing at the Faculty of Computer Science and Electrical Engineering at the University of Rostock. Its research focuses on the integration of symbolic, probabilistic, and neural approaches in AI & ML. The application domains include AI-supported digital twins, intelligent environments, situation-aware assistance, and multimodal diagnostics. The methodological focus is on sequential state estimation using hybrid Bayesian and neuro-symbolic models, as well as the integration of deep learning with symbolic background knowledge. The positions are co-supervised by Hessian.AI group leader Martin Becker (Marburg University) focusing on knowledge-centric AI and biomedical ML with a strong international network.

We look forward to receiving your application!

Universität Rostock
18051 Rostock
Tel.: +49 381 498 - 0

Universitätsplatz 1
18055 Rostock

description:
A Snapshot of Your Day

As a Lead Digitalization Expert (f/m/d) within Digital Operations (GT SO DO), focusing on Tender & Project Management within Grid Solutions for large infrastructure High Voltage Direct Current (HVDC) and Grid Access (GA) projects, you are the hands-on digital partner shaping how these projects are prepared, handed over, and executed. You work closely with Tendering, Project Management, and Project Controls to simplify core workflows, improve data consistency, and increase transparency across the tender-to-project lifecycle.

Your day is shaped by supporting the business in tackling real execution challenges typical for large-scale, multi-year projects: complex tool landscapes, inconsistent data across project phases and regions, manual processes, and limited end-to-end transparency across tendering, project, and site activities. You define and own the digital strategy for tender and project management of highly complex HVDC/GA projects and actively turn it into robust, feasible, and sustainable digital solutions.

Working at the intersection of Digital Operations, HV business teams, and IT, you ensure that digital solutions are embedded into daily tender and project management routines and deliver measurable impact for ongoing and future projects.

How You’ll Make an Impact
Lead the digitalization of tender and project management processes in the High Voltage environment, focusing on tender preparation, project handover, execution transparency, and reporting
Drive the implementation of digital solutions and process improvements across Tendering and Project Management, ensuring alignment with TPS standards, DO guardrails, and the end to end project operating model
Enable collaboration and adoption by facilitating targeted workshops with Tender Managers, Project Managers, and Project Controls to embed digital solutions into day to day work
Strengthen data foundations and literacy by improving the consistency, structure, and reuse of tender and project data across phases and systems
Identify and prioritize high value digital opportunities that reduce manual effort, improve handover quality, and increase predictability in HV projects
Collaborate cross functionally with Digital Operations, IT, Engineering, Procurement, Logistics, and Site Management to ensure integrated digital workflows across the full project lifecycle
Provide digital expertise to support Tendering and Project Management in shaping solutions that are aligned, and future-proof across long project lifecycles
What You Bring
Degree in Engineering, Project Management, Business Administration, Information Systems, or a comparable field
Several years of relevant work experience in tendering, project management, or digitalization within complex project or High Voltage environments
Strong understanding of tender to project workflows and the practical needs of Project Managers and Tender Leads
Experience with project management tools, data structures, and digital solutions supporting project execution is an advantage
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills for effective cross‑functional collaboration
Structured, pragmatic, and hands‑on working style with a strong focus on operational impact
About the Team

Our Grid Technology division enables a reliable, sustainable, and digital grid. The power grid is the backbone of the energy transition. Siemens Energy offers a leading portfolio and solutions in HVDC transmission, grid stabilization and storage, high voltage switchgears and transformers, and digital grid technology.

Who is Siemens Energy?

At Siemens Energy, we are more than just an energy technology company. With ~100.000 dedicated employees in more than 90 countries, we develop the energy systems of the future, ensuring that the growing energy demand of the global community is met reliably and sustainably. The technologies created in our research departments and factories drive the energy transition and provide the base for one sixth of the world's electricity generation.

Our global team is committed to making sustainable, reliable, and affordable energy a reality by pushing the boundaries of what is possible. We uphold a 150-year legacy of innovation that encourages our search for people who will support our focus on decarbonization, new technologies, and energy transformation.

Find out how you can make a difference at Siemens Energy: https://www.siemens-energy.com/employeevideo

Our Commitment to Diversity

Lucky for us, we are not all the same. Through diversity we generate power. We run on inclusion and our combined creative energy is fueled by over 130 nationalities. Siemens Energy celebrates character – no matter what ethnic background, gender, age, religion, identity, or disability. We energize society, all of society, and we do not discriminate based on our differences.

Rewards/Benefits
In addition to an attractive remuneration package in line with the market, you can expect an attractive employer-financed company pension scheme
We also offer the opportunity to become a Siemens Energy shareholder
We offer our employees the opportunity to work flexibly and remotely, and our inspiring offices provide space for collaboration and creativity
The professional and personal development of our employees is very important to us. We provide them with the opportunities to learn and develop in a self-determined way, various attractive programmes and learning materials are available for this purpose
In relation to the "compatibility of family and work", we have a wide range of offers, e.g. flexible working time models, childcare places at many locations, the possibility of trial part-time work or even a sabbatical
We value equal opportunities and welcome applications from people with disabilities.

https://jobs.siemens-energy.com/jobs

description:
Standort: ERL S SP 11

Art der Anstellung: Fixed Term / Part-time

Momentaufnahme von deinem Tag

Du unterstützt den Bereich Tender & Special Fields innerhalb der Einheit Plant Layout & Civil Engineering. Zur Abdeckung dieses Bedarfs konzentrierst du dich auf zwei Kernbereiche: die technische und projektbezogene Unterstützung, einschließlich der Mitwirkung an Ausschreibungsunterlagen sowie einfachen ingenieurtechnischen Tätigkeiten, sowie die administrative und organisatorische Unterstützung, etwa im Dokumentenmanagement und bei allgemeinen Aufgaben zur Teamunterstützung. Diese Aufgabenstruktur stellt sicher, dass du einen echten Mehrwert für das Team leistest und gleichzeitig wertvolle praktische Berufserfahrung sammelst.

Wie du etwas bewirken kannst

Unterstützung bei der Erstellung und Prüfung technischer Dokumentationen für Projektausschreibungen und Angebote
Durchführung grundlegender Berechnungen und Datenanalysen zur Unterstützung von Bauingenieurprojekten
Mitwirkung bei der Erstellung und Aktualisierung technischer Zeichnungen, Layouts und Pläne unter Anleitung erfahrener Ingenieur:innen
Zusammenstellung und Aufbereitung technischer Daten, Spezifikationen und Materialinformationen für die Projektplanung
Pflege, Strukturierung und Organisation der Projektdokumentation (Berichte, Zeichnungen, Korrespondenz) in digitalen Archiven
Unterstützung bei Präsentationen, Berichten, Protokollen sowie allgemeinen administrativen Aufgaben zur Sicherstellung eines reibungslosen Tagesgeschäfts

Was du mitbringst

Derzeitiges Bachelor‑ oder Masterstudium im Bauingenieurwesen oder in einem eng verwandten Studiengang
Einstiegsposition für Studierende ohne erforderliche vorherige Berufserfahrung
Grundlegende theoretische Kenntnisse der Bauingenieur‑ und Tragwerkslehre aus dem universitären Studium
Fachliche Grundkompetenzen in bauingenieurtechnischen Prinzipien, technischer Dokumentation und Datenanalyse
Anwendungskenntnisse gängiger Softwaretools, insbesondere MS Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) sowie grundlegende CAD‑Kenntnisse
Persönliche und methodische Fähigkeiten wie Organisationsstärke, Detailgenauigkeit, Kommunikationsfähigkeit sowie eine ausgeprägte Lernbereitschaft und Eigeninitiative

Über das Team

Unser Geschäftsbereich Grid Technologies ermöglicht ein zuverlässiges, nachhaltiges und digitales Netz. Das Stromnetz bildet das Rückgrat der Energiewende. Siemens Energy bietet ein führendes Portfolio und Lösungen in den Bereichen HGÜ-Technik, Netzstabilisierung und Stromspeicherung, Hochspannungsschaltgeräte und Transformatoren sowie digitale Netztechnologie.

Wer ist Siemens Energy?

Wir bei Siemens Energy sind mehr als nur ein Energietechnologieunternehmen. Mit ~100,000 engagierten Mitarbeitern in mehr als 90 Ländern entwickeln wir die Energiesysteme der Zukunft und stellen sicher, dass der wachsende Energiebedarf der Weltgemeinschaft zuverlässig und nachhaltig gedeckt wird. Die in unseren Forschungsabteilungen und Fabriken entstehenden Technologien treiben die Energiewende voran und bilden die Grundlage für ein Sechstel der weltweiten Stromerzeugung.

Unser globales Team setzt sich dafür ein, nachhaltige, zuverlässige und bezahlbare Energie Wirklichkeit werden zu lassen, indem es die Grenzen des Möglichen immer weiter hinausschiebt. Wir blicken zurück auf eine 150-jährige Tradition der Innovation, die unsere Suche nach Mitarbeitern bestärkt, die unseren Fokus auf Dekarbonisierung, neue Technologien und Energiewandel unterstützen.

Erfahre mehr darüber, wie du bei Siemens Energy etwas bewegen kannst: https://www.siemens-energy.com/employeevideo

Unser Engagement für Vielfalt

Gut, dass wir nicht alle gleich sind. Vielfalt macht uns leistungsstark. Wir leben von Inklusion und unsere gemeinsame kreative Energie wird von über 130 Nationalitäten gespeist. Siemens Energy setzt auf den Charakter - unabhängig von ethnischem Hintergrund, Geschlecht, Alter, Religion, Identität oder Behinderung. Wir versorgen die Gesellschaft mit Energie - die ganze Gesellschaft - und wir diskriminieren nicht aufgrund von Unterschieden, die zwischen uns bestehen.

Benefits

Spannende Einblicke in ein internationales Unternehmen
Lege mit uns den Grundstein für deine Karriere

Wir legen Wert auf Chancengleichheit und freuen uns über Bewerbungen von Menschen mit Behinderung.

https://jobs.siemens-energy.com/jobs

Momentaufnahme von deinem Tag

Du unterstützt den Bereich Tender & Special Fields innerhalb der Einheit Plant Layout & Civil Engineering. Zur Abdeckung dieses Bedarfs konzentrierst du dich auf zwei Kernbereiche: die technische und projektbezogene Unterstützung, einschließlich der Mitwirkung an Ausschreibungsunterlagen sowie einfachen ingenieurtechnischen Tätigkeiten, sowie die administrative und organisatorische Unterstützung, etwa im Dokumentenmanagement und bei allgemeinen Aufgaben zur Teamunterstützung. Diese Aufgabenstruktur stellt sicher, dass du einen echten Mehrwert für das Team leistest und gleichzeitig wertvolle praktische Berufserfahrung sammelst.

Wie du etwas bewirken kannst

  • Unterstützung bei der Erstellung und Prüfung technischer Dokumentationen für Projektausschreibungen und Angebote
  • Durchführung grundlegender Berechnungen und Datenanalysen zur Unterstützung von Bauingenieurprojekten
  • Mitwirkung bei der Erstellung und Aktualisierung technischer Zeichnungen, Layouts und Pläne unter Anleitung erfahrener Ingenieur:innen
  • Zusammenstellung und Aufbereitung technischer Daten, Spezifikationen und Materialinformationen für die Projektplanung
  • Pflege, Strukturierung und Organisation der Projektdokumentation (Berichte, Zeichnungen, Korrespondenz) in digitalen Archiven
  • Unterstützung bei Präsentationen, Berichten, Protokollen sowie allgemeinen administrativen Aufgaben zur Sicherstellung eines reibungslosen Tagesgeschäfts

Was du mitbringst

  • Derzeitiges Bachelor‑ oder Masterstudium im Bauingenieurwesen oder in einem eng verwandten Studiengang
  • Einstiegsposition für Studierende ohne erforderliche vorherige Berufserfahrung
  • Grundlegende theoretische Kenntnisse der Bauingenieur‑ und Tragwerkslehre aus dem universitären Studium
  • Fachliche Grundkompetenzen in bauingenieurtechnischen Prinzipien, technischer Dokumentation und Datenanalyse
  • Anwendungskenntnisse gängiger Softwaretools, insbesondere MS Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) sowie grundlegende CAD‑Kenntnisse
  • Persönliche und methodische Fähigkeiten wie Organisationsstärke, Detailgenauigkeit, Kommunikationsfähigkeit sowie eine ausgeprägte Lernbereitschaft und Eigeninitiative

Über das Team

Unser Geschäftsbereich Grid Technologies ermöglicht ein zuverlässiges, nachhaltiges und digitales Netz. Das Stromnetz bildet das Rückgrat der Energiewende. Siemens Energy bietet ein führendes Portfolio und Lösungen in den Bereichen HGÜ-Technik, Netzstabilisierung und Stromspeicherung, Hochspannungsschaltgeräte und Transformatoren sowie digitale Netztechnologie.

Wer ist Siemens Energy?

Wir bei Siemens Energy sind mehr als nur ein Energietechnologieunternehmen. Mit ~100,000 engagierten Mitarbeitern in mehr als 90 Ländern entwickeln wir die Energiesysteme der Zukunft und stellen sicher, dass der wachsende Energiebedarf der Weltgemeinschaft zuverlässig und nachhaltig gedeckt wird. Die in unseren Forschungsabteilungen und Fabriken entstehenden Technologien treiben die Energiewende voran und bilden die Grundlage für ein Sechstel der weltweiten Stromerzeugung.

Unser globales Team setzt sich dafür ein, nachhaltige, zuverlässige und bezahlbare Energie Wirklichkeit werden zu lassen, indem es die Grenzen des Möglichen immer weiter hinausschiebt. Wir blicken zurück auf eine 150-jährige Tradition der Innovation, die unsere Suche nach Mitarbeitern bestärkt, die unseren Fokus auf Dekarbonisierung, neue Technologien und Energiewandel unterstützen.

Erfahre mehr darüber, wie du bei Siemens Energy etwas bewegen kannst: https://www.siemens-energy.com/employeevideo

Unser Engagement für Vielfalt

Gut, dass wir nicht alle gleich sind. Vielfalt macht uns leistungsstark. Wir leben von Inklusion und unsere gemeinsame kreative Energie wird von über 130 Nationalitäten gespeist. Siemens Energy setzt auf den Charakter - unabhängig von ethnischem Hintergrund, Geschlecht, Alter, Religion, Identität oder Behinderung. Wir versorgen die Gesellschaft mit Energie - die ganze Gesellschaft - und wir diskriminieren nicht aufgrund von Unterschieden, die zwischen uns bestehen.

Benefits

  • Spannende Einblicke in ein internationales Unternehmen
  • Lege mit uns den Grundstein für deine Karriere

Wir legen Wert auf Chancengleichheit und freuen uns über Bewerbungen von Menschen mit Behinderung.

https://jobs.siemens-energy.com/jobs

companyInfo/companyDescription: Siemens Gamesa brings together a team of over 25,000 passionate employees of close to 100 nationalities working in more than 90 countries around the world. This diversity and breadth is a tremendous advantage, as employees benefit from one another’s experience and technical know-how. Our global reach opens new horizons for professional opportunities and growth, and our work has a bigger impact than ever on the ongoing transformation of the energy industry.
description:
A Snapshot of your Day

As a Working Student in the Drivetrain & Mechanicals for O&M team at Siemens Gamesa Renewable Energy, you will support the Global Commodity (GCM) Mechanical team on various operational and strategic tasks. Your role will involve assisting with procurement-related projects, supporting the analysis and maintenance of offers and contracts, and contributing to day-to-day operations in service procurement. A passion for renewable energy and a sense of the importance of leading change are essential.

How You'll Make an Impact

Support Operations Management in daily tasks and assist the on-site team with technical tasks
Maintain reports and prepare slide decks for meetings
Organize project documentation, operational records, and team files
Participate in supplier management processes, including onboarding and evaluation
Assist in managing order processes for materials and help with team events
Contribute to the preparation of tenders and negotiations

What You Bring

Currently enrolled in a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree program in Engineering, Business Administration, or a related field
Strong analytical skills and proficiency in Microsoft Excel, PowerPoint, and Word
Excellent written and verbal communication in English; German is a plus
Demonstrated interest in wind power or broader renewables

Who is Siemens Gamesa?

Siemens Gamesa is part of Siemens Energy, a global leader in energy technology with a rich legacy of innovation spanning over 150 years. Together, we are committed to making sustainable, reliable, and affordable energy a reality by pushing the boundaries of what is possible. As a leading player in the wind industry and manufacturer of wind turbines, we are passionate about driving the energy transition and providing innovative solutions that meet the growing energy demand of the global community. At Siemens Gamesa, we are constantly seeking talented individuals to join our team and support our focus on energy transformation.

Our Commitment to Diversity

Lucky for us, we are not all the same. Through diversity we generate power. We run on inclusion and our combined creative energy is fueled by over 130 nationalities. Siemens Energy celebrates character – no matter what ethnic background, gender, age, religion, identity, or disability. We energize society, all of society, and we do not discriminate based on our differences.

Rewards/Benefits

Become a part of our mission for sustainability: Clean energy for generations to come
A global team of diverse colleagues who share passion for renewable energy
Trust and empowerment to make your own ideas reality
Flexible working hours as well as home-office
Local benefits such as subsided lunch and public transport tickets, employee discounts and much more
Exciting insights into an international company
Lay the foundation for your career with us

Severely disabled applicants are encouraged to reach out to us. We have inclusive recruiting processes specifically for severely disabled persons in Germany and we do our utmost to tailor working spaces to suit your individual needs. In cases of severe disability, applicants who are equally qualified will be given preferential consideration

description:
Snapshot of Your Day

Obsolescence is a key risk in the project and lifecycle business of complex energy solutions. In your role as Obsolescence Manager (m/f/d), you actively shape the setup and firm integration of effective obsolescence management for HVDC /FACTS / BESS solutions. You operate daily at the intersection of strategy, processes, and execution, ensuring that obsolescence information is flowing and risks can be identified early, transparently assessed, and consistently addressed. You will ensure lifecycle continuity, minimize operational and commercial risk, and support long‑term customer commitments for our HVDC and FACTS portfolio.

How You’ll Make an Impact
Establish a holistic obsolescence management framework for HVDC / FACTS / BESS solutions and continuously develop the overarching obsolescence strategy and internal processes.
Anchor obsolescence management across all relevant areas of the Solutions organization, particularly in Purchasing, Sales, Tender Management, Engineering, Project Management, and R&D.
Define and take ownership of clear roles, interfaces, and governance structures to ensure effective obsolescence management across the organization.
Drive the further development of existing internal obsolescence processes and take responsibility for their consistent application in tenders and customer projects.
What You Bring
Successfully completed degree in Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Industrial Engineering, or a comparable technical discipline.
Several years of professional experience in project business with complex, customer-specific solutions and long product lifecycles, ideally in the energy, HVDC, FACTS or BESS environment.
Strong expertise in project leadership as well as project and stakeholder management, including the confident coordination of cross-functional topics between Purchasing, Sales, Tender Management, Engineering, and R&D.
Experience in obsolescence management, lifecycle management, supply chain management, risk management, or comparable functions.
Strong understanding of processes and governance, as well as experience in building, further developing, and applying processes in complex organizational environments.
Well-developed change-management capabilities, assertiveness, high level of ownership, and confident interaction with internal stakeholders across different hierarchy levels.
About the Team

Our Grid Technology division enables a reliable, sustainable, and digital grid. The power grid is the backbone of the energy transition. Siemens Energy offers a leading portfolio and solutions in HVDC transmission, grid stabilization and storage, high voltage switchgears and transformers, and digital grid technology.

Who is Siemens Energy?

At Siemens Energy, we are more than just an energy technology company. With ~100.000 dedicated employees in more than 90 countries, we develop the energy systems of the future, ensuring that the growing energy demand of the global community is met reliably and sustainably. The technologies created in our research departments and factories drive the energy transition and provide the base for one sixth of the world's electricity generation.

Our global team is committed to making sustainable, reliable, and affordable energy a reality by pushing the boundaries of what is possible. We uphold a 150-year legacy of innovation that encourages our search for people who will support our focus on decarbonization, new technologies, and energy transformation.

Find out how you can make a difference at Siemens Energy: https://www.siemens-energy.com/employeevideo

Our Commitment to Diversity

Lucky for us, we are not all the same. Through diversity we generate power. We run on inclusion and our combined creative energy is fueled by over 130 nationalities. Siemens Energy celebrates character – no matter what ethnic background, gender, age, religion, identity, or disability. We energize society, all of society, and we do not discriminate based on our differences.

Rewards/Benefits
In addition to an attractive remuneration package in line with the market, you can expect an attractive employer-financed company pension scheme
We also offer the opportunity to become a Siemens Energy shareholder
We offer our employees the opportunity to work flexibly and remotely, and our inspiring offices provide space for collaboration and creativity
The professional and personal development of our employees is very important to us. We provide them with the opportunities to learn and develop in a self-determined way, various attractive programmes and learning materials are available for this purpose
In relation to the "compatibility of family and work", we have a wide range of offers, e.g. flexible working time models, childcare places at many locations, the possibility of trial part-time work or even a sabbatical
We value equal opportunities and welcome applications from people with disabilities.

https://jobs.siemens-energy.com/jobs
