Strategic Buyer - Indirect Procurement (m/f/d)
Area of activity
Finance
Location
Eschborn (Headquarter)
Entry level
Professional
Contract type
Unlimited
descriptionHTML:
Engineered in Germany, Proven in the Wild

Jack Wolfskin is an internationally renowned outdoor brand and part of the ANTA Sports Group, one of the world’s leading sporting goods companies. As one of the top providers of high-quality outdoor apparel, footwear, and equipment in Europe and China, Jack Wolfskin stands for innovation, functionality, and sustainability. With a strong network of owned stores and franchise partners, the company is also one of the largest franchisors in Germany's sporting goods retail sector.

We’re looking for people with a spirit of adventure - free thinkers who blaze their own trail and explore the wild with passion together with a strong team that lives and breathes the outdoors. Sound like you? Then welcome to the Wild Ones.

We free the wild inside everyone and out there!
We are looking for you to join our team in the Procurement Department at our headquarters in Idstein as a Strategic Buyer - Indirect Procurement (m/f/d) as soon as possible.
Our company headquarters will relocate to Eschborn towards the end of 2026, and your primary workplace will transition to Eschborn accordingly.
Your role in the Wolfpack:

Category Sourcing & Negotiation

  • Independently lead sourcing and negotiation activities across various non-trade categories, including:
  • IT systems and services
  • Legal and compliance service providers
  • Office services, facility management, and lease agreements
  • Marketing, travel, and other professional services
  • Support the Director in managing tender processes, vendor evaluations, and the negotiation and finalization of commercial agreements
  • Collaborate cross-functionally with legal, finance, and other departments to ensure contracts align with internal policies and legal, financial, and risk requirements

Process Implementation & Optimization

  • Contribute to the design, rollout, and continuous improvement of procurement processes, tools, and approval workflows
  • Assist in developing and maintaining standardized templates for RFPs, contracts, and purchase orders
  • Establish and maintain systems for contract documentation, archiving, and performance tracking
  • Ensure adherence to procurement guidelines and proactively address any process deviations

Stakeholder Engagement & Enablement

  • Act as a trusted point of contact for internal teams engaging with external suppliers and service providers
  • Advise stakeholders on procurement procedures, digital tools, and approval workflows
  • Support training initiatives and foster the adoption of procurement best practices across the organization

Vendor Management & Performance Monitoring

  • Oversee operational relationships with selected non-trade suppliers to ensure service delivery and compliance
  • Monitor supplier performance, key performance indicators (KPIs), and adherence to contractual obligations
  • Coordinate contract renewals, amendments, and supplier evaluations in partnership with relevant departments
  • Use market intelligence and spend analysis to support strategic sourcing decisions in alignment with the Director
What you bring:
  • Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Procurement, Supply Chain Management, or a related discipline
  • Minimum of 5 years of professional experience in indirect (non-trade) procurement, preferably within the consumer goods or retail sector
  • In-depth knowledge of procurement operations, supplier management, and sourcing strategies
  • Strong negotiation skills and experience managing service-based contracts
  • Familiarity with procurement systems and digital workflows,SAP S/4HANA experience is an advantage
  • Highly organized and detail-oriented with a strong focus on process optimization
  • Excellent interpersonal skills and experience managing internal and external stakeholders
  • Fluent in German and English (written and spoken) additional language skills are a plus
What you can expect from us:
  • An exciting product world and international environment in a responsible, sustainability-driven company
  • German and English as our working languages
  • 30 days of paid vacation per year
  • Flexible and hybrid working models
  • Mobile working from abroad for up to 4 weeks per year
  • Attractive employee discounts and company benefits
  • “Employee Referral” bonus program
  • Exclusive perks via Corporate Benefits
  • Bike leasing – monthly subsidies for up to two leasing contracts
  • EGYM company fitness – access to over 7,000 gyms
  • Team events and outdoor sports activities
  • A first-name culture and casual dress code
  • Free water, coffee, and a variety of teas
Ready for your next adventure?
Then become part of our team! We look forward to receiving your application – exclusively via our online career portal – including your CV and your earliest possible start date.


Job Details

Title
Strategic Buyer - Indirect Procurement (m/f/d)
Area of activity
Finance
Location
Eschborn (Headquarter)
Entry level
Professional
Contract type
Unlimited

companyInfo/companyDescription:
Mit über 100.000 engagierten Arbeitnehmern in mehr als 90 Ländern entwickeln wir die Energiesysteme der Zukunft und stellen den wachsenden Energiebedarf der Menschen weltweit verlässlich und nachhaltig sicher.

Durch die in unseren Forschungsabteilungen und Fabriken entwickelten Technologien treiben wir die Energiewende voran und schaffen die Grundlage für ein Sechstel der weltweiten Energieerzeugung. Unser weltweites Team arbeitet daran, nachhaltige, zuverlässige und erschwingliche Energie Wirklichkeit werden zu lassen, indem es die Grenzen des Möglichen immer weiter verschiebt.

Seit über 150 Jahren stehen wir für Innovation und suchen nach Menschen, die uns dabei helfen, die Dekarbonisierung, neue Technologien und die Energiewende schwerpunktmäßig voranzutreiben. Möglichkeiten entdecken, um bei Siemens Energy einen Beitrag zu leisten.
description:
A Snapshot of Your Day

As a Gas Turbine Control System Expert (f/m/d), you will be part of our global product development team responsible for the automation of our gas turbine packages. In this role, you will work closely with our global bid and project engineering teams.

How You’ll Make an Impact

Functional coordination of the team and technical responsibility for standard engineering of turbine control and protection
Further development of technological leadership and contribution to supplier development for turbine control and protection components in line with gas turbine technology requirements
Identification and implementation of cost reduction measures, including active knowledge exchange with relevant stakeholders
Advisory support to development management, gas turbine engineering, sales, and project execution on technical topics
Support of projects involving complex technical solutions and consulting during bid preparation, including tender and specification analysis
Responsibility for the maintenance and further development of internal engineering tools

What You Bring

University degree in engineering (preferably electrical engineering) or a comparable qualification
Extensive professional experience in turbine automation, with strong knowledge of control systems, turbine protection, and gas turbine process engineering
In-depth knowledge of automation and control systems, ideally Omnivise T3000
Solid knowledge of failure mode and effects analysis (FMEA), functional safety, and programming skills (e.g., Visual Basic)
Strong leadership and influencing skills, combined with creativity and a conceptual approach to technical solutions
Very good English language skills

About the Team

Our Gas Services division offers low-emission power generation through service and decarbonization. We cover zero- or low-emission power generation, gas turbines, steam turbines, and generators under one roof. We also provide decarbonization opportunities through service offerings, modernization, and digitalization of the fleet.

Who is Siemens Energy?

At Siemens Energy, we are more than just an energy technology company. With approximately 100,000 dedicated employees in more than 90 countries, we develop the energy systems of the future, ensuring that the growing global energy demand is met reliably and sustainably. The technologies created in our research departments and factories drive the energy transition and provide the foundation for one sixth of the world's electricity generation.

Our global team is committed to making sustainable, reliable, and affordable energy a reality by pushing the boundaries of what is possible. We build on a 150-year legacy of innovation that drives our search for people who support our focus on decarbonization, new technologies, and energy transformation.

Find out how you can make a difference at Siemens Energy:

https://www.siemens-energy.com/employeevideo

Our Commitment to Diversity

Lucky for us, we are not all the same. Through diversity, we generate power. We run on inclusion, and our combined creative energy is fueled by more than 130 nationalities. Siemens Energy celebrates character—no matter ethnicity, gender, age, religion, identity, or disability. We energize society—all of society—and do not discriminate based on our differences.

Benefits

In addition to an attractive remuneration package in line with the market, you can expect an employer-financed company pension scheme
We also offer the opportunity to become a Siemens Energy shareholder under very attractive employee conditions
Under our motto #BetterTogether, we offer flexible and remote working options, as well as modern office spaces that foster collaboration and creativity
The professional and personal development of our employees is very important to us. We offer various programs and initiatives to support self-directed learning and development
To support work-life balance, we offer a wide range of benefits, such as flexible working time models, childcare options at many locations, trial part-time arrangements, and sabbaticals
We value equal opportunities and welcome applications from people with disabilities.

https://jobs.siemens-energy.com/jobs

companyInfo/companyDescription:
Rohde & Schwarz is a global technology group striving for a safer and connected world. With
its Test & Measurement, Technology Systems and Networks & Cybersecurity Divisions, the
company creates tomorrow's innovations today. The company's leading-edge products and
solutions empower industrial, regulatory and military customers to attain technological and
digital sovereignty.

The privately owned company is known for stability and resilience. Independence is at the
core of its entrepreneurial identity. The company finances its growth with its own resources.
Because the company does not have to think in terms of quarterly results, it can plan sustainably for the long term. The high vertical integration of Rohde & Schwarz makes the
company a reliable, trustworthy and relevant partner for its customers. 

On June 30, 2025, Rohde & Schwarz had more than 15,000 employees worldwide. The group generated net revenue of EUR 3.16 billion in the 2024/2025 fiscal year (July to June).

Kontakt

Robert Nienke
Recruiting Manager

Info
Stadt/Region Dresden (Deutschland)Einstiegslevel Berufserfahrene, PromovierteBeschäftigungsart Vollzeit, unbefristetRef. Nummer 15252

Your tasks
With your affinity for complex systems you will drive the new space based solutions, working closely with colleagues, that are experts in their fields, and external organizations
You create and maintain technical product materials such as product brochures, flyers, whitepapers, solution briefs, presentations and web content for our Space Solution systems
You translate complex technical concepts and system capabilities into well-structured, understandable and audience-appropriate texts for government, defence and industry customers
You work closely with Product Management, Systems Engineering, Sales and Marketing colleagues to ensure technically accurate and consistent content
You structure and present information on missions, use cases, system architecture (space, ground and user segment) and key performance features
You develop and maintain templates, style guides and terminology (glossary)
You contribute to the content development of tender documents, customer presentations and campaign materials
You ensure a professional and consistent representation of our space system solutions across all media and document types
Exciting insights into Rohde & Schwarz

Our colleagues provide insider information about:

Daily adventures and challenges
Our passionate team
The technologies behind the innovative projects and solutions
Your qualifications
University degree in degree in Technical Communication/Technical Writing, Engineering, Computer Science, Physics, Communications Engineering, Aerospace Engineering or a comparable qualification
Several years of experience in creating technical documentation and/or marketing-oriented technical content (e.g. in B2B technology, defence, space, telecommunications or similar fields)
Strong technical understanding, ideally with exposure to space, SIGINT, ISR, EW or SatCom
Knowledge of (space based) radiofrequency communications (transmitter, receivers, antenna, satellite communications, mobile phone communications) and satellite platforms
Excellent ability to structure complex topics and express them in clear, concise and readable texts
Experience with common office tools (e.g. Word, PowerPoint) and ideally with content or document management tools
Can-do mentality and willingness to travel internationally within intercultural and interdisciplinary environments
Fluent in English and German
Interested?
We are looking forward to receiving your application! Ideally, you should apply online with the reference number. If you have any questions, please feel free to contact your recruiting contact via or .

Equal opportunities are important to us. We are looking forward to receiving your application regardless of gender, nationality, ethnic and social origin, religion, ideology, disability, age as well as sexual orientation and identity.

Reference number: 15252
Recruiting contact: Robert Nienke

At Rohde & Schwarz, we develop and manufacture solutions in the areas of Test & Measurement, Technology Systems, and Networks & Cybersecurity – driven by the vision of a secure and connected world. From software to final production, our more than 15,000 employees design products and systems that safeguard the technological sovereignty of our customers from industry, government, and sovereign sectors, while setting global standards. Our headquarters is centrally located in Munich and also serves as our main development site.

companyInfo/companyDescription:
Tesla is accelerating the world’s transition to sustainable energy. We design, manufacture, sell, install and service the world’s best solar technology, energy storage systems, and electric vehicles, providing customers the opportunity to generate, store and consume energy entirely sustainably.
 
Tesla is committed to hiring and developing top talent from around the world for any given discipline. Based in California, Tesla’s workforce spans across four continents. We work to build an inclusive environment in which all people, regardless of gender, race, religion, or background, can come to do their best work. 
 
Our world-class teams operate with a non-conventional philosophy of inter-disciplinary collaboration. Each member of the team is expected to challenge and to be challenged, to create, and to innovate. We’re tackling the world’s most difficult and important problems—and we wouldn’t succeed without our shared passion for making the world a better place.
description:
What to Expect

Tesla is seeking a talented Senior Account Manager to join our growing EMEA Megapack sales team.

As a Senior Account Manager, you will lead the customer engagement, sales, and contracting efforts for our cutting-edge Megapack battery energy storage systems and industry-leading services. By strengthening our energy infrastructure with Megapack, we create a cleaner and more stable grid.

From conception to contract execution, you will close complex, multimillion-dollar deals, leading project teams with colleagues from engineering, operations, finance, service, and legal. To be successful in this role, you will bring strong technical knowledge of the utility-scale electricity industry and a proven track record of leading complex contract negotiations.

What You'll Do
Drive negotiation and contracting of equipment supply agreements, EPC agreements, O&M agreements, and related software sales agreements
Achieve sales goals by managing a project pipeline of opportunities spanning multiple countries, timelines and project types
Develop sales opportunities by building and maintaining relationships with Independent Power Producers (IPPs), developers, and utilities
Collaborate with internal stakeholders to define scope, terms, and pricing for battery storage projects meeting customers’ needs while protecting Tesla’s interests
Contribute to the strategy for and preparation of tender responses, in collaboration with Tesla’s Bid Team
Ensure accurate pipeline data and provide reporting to management
Evaluate market dynamics and drivers. Provide key market insights to the broader Tesla team, determining and executing the most effective go-to-market strategies

What You'll Bring
7+ years of sales/account management experience in the utility-scale electric generation, transmission, or storage industry
Demonstrated expertise in structuring and leading complex negotiations in energy, construction, project finance, or a related field. Experience in renewable energy industry is a plus
Ability to identify problems, lead projects, and provide pragmatic solutions
Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to build trust with colleagues and customers
Flexibility to travel (up to 50%)
Fluency in English and German

Tesla is an Equal Opportunity / Affirmative Action employer committed to diversity in the workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, age, national origin, disability, protected veteran status, gender identity or any other factor protected by applicable federal, state or local laws.

Tesla is also committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to individuals with disabilities. Please let your recruiter know if you need an accommodation at any point during the interview process.

companyInfo/companyDescription:
Die Universität Rostock wurde im Jahre 1419 gegründet und ist damit eine der ältesten Hochschulen Deutschlands sowie die älteste Universität im Ostseeraum.



Im Wintersemester 2021/22 waren 12.665 Studierende immatrikuliert, darunter auch viele Erasmus- und weitere Auslandsstudenten. 2018 lehrten in Rostock 275 Professoren. Insgesamt werden von der Universität etwas mehr als 2.900 Mitarbeiter (darunter ca. 850 in der Universitätsmedizin) direkt beschäftigt. Die Mehrheit der über 100 Studiengänge in zehn Fakultäten wird mit Master- und Bachelorabschlüssen angeboten. Die Universität Rostock gilt auf Grund ihres umfangreichen Studienangebots als Volluniversität. In einem 2018 veröffentlichten Ranking von Times Higher Education wurde die Hochschule als die schönste Universität Deutschlands und die viertschönste Europas gelistet.



Hauptgebäude der Universität Rostock

Mehrere Nobelpreisträger haben in Rostock studiert oder geforscht, darunter Albrecht Kossel, Karl von Frisch und Otto Stern. Im Umfeld der Universität haben sich durch Institute, Gründungen und Unternehmensansiedlungen Cluster der Wirtschaft und Forschung in Bereichen der Spitzentechnologie gebildet. Vor allem in den Sektoren Biotechnologie, Medizin und Lebenswissenschaften (zum Beispiel BioCon Valley und DZNE), Nanotechnologie, in der Lichtforschung und Photonik, Agrar- und Umweltwissenschaften, in der maritimen Forschung (IOW) sowie in der Erforschung von Bereichen der Datenverarbeitung und Informationsgesellschaft (Fraunhofer-Institut für Graphische Datenverarbeitung) haben sich bedeutende Vertreter angesiedelt. Auch die demografische Forschung im Umfeld der Rostocker Universität, die über mehrere Sonderforschungsbereiche verfügt, ist von Weltrang, vor allem mit dem zuständigen Max-Planck-Institut.
description:
The University of Rostock offers a diverse, varied and challenging position in a tradition-conscious, yet innovative, modern and family-friendly university in a lively city by the sea.

At the Faculty of Computer Science and Electrical Engineering, Institute for Visual and Analytic Computing, subject to allocation of funds, we are filling the following position at the earliest possible date on a temporary basis for the duration of the project Nexcell ending on 31.12.2028:

Research Assistant - Doctoral or Postdoctoral (m/f/d) - AI

Start date at the earliest possible date

Working hours full-time with 40 hours

Remuneration pay group 13 TV-L

Location Rostock

Tender number P 65/2026

Limitation limited until 31.12.2028

Application time 2026-06-15

Please do not hesitate to contact us for further information:

HR department:
Pia-Lucy Dahl
Phone number: 0381/498-1291
E-mail:

Department:
Prof. Dr.-Ing. Thomas Kirste
Phone number: 0381/498-7510
E-mail:

NEXCELL is a major multi-million-€ collaborative research initiative that brings together leading industrial and academic partners to create a groundbreaking point-of-care platform for next-generation cell and gene therapies. The vision is to enable personalized cancer treatments to be manufactured directly at the clinical site – making life-saving therapies more accessible and scalable. Within this project, the University of Rostock serves as the AI technology provider, developing a probabilistic digital twin of the NEXCELL bioreactor system. This digital twin will combine Bayesian AI, deep learning, symbolic reasoning, and hybrid modeling techniques to intelligently monitor and predict both technical and biological processes. By addressing challenges such as uncertainty quantification, multimodal sensor fusion, anomaly detection, and robust state estimation, our research will push the frontiers of AI in complex, safety-critical, and data-sparse domains. For motivated AI researchers, NEXCELL offers a unique opportunity to conduct fundamental research at the interface of cutting-edge machine learning and real-world bioprocess applications, with the potential for high-impact publications, open-source contributions, and direct collaboration with a market leader in bioreactor technology.

We invite an outstanding doctoral or postdoctoral researcher to join our institute in collaboration with a global industrial leader in bioreactor technology. The successful candidate will contribute to a large-scale, interdisciplinary research project focused on the development and deployment of advanced artificial intelligence methods for system monitoring, state estimation, and decision support in next-generation bioreactor platforms.

The project addresses theoretical and applied challenges at the intersection of machine learning, probabilistic modeling, symbolic AI, single cell analysis, and bioprocess engineering, with strong relevance to both academic research and industrial innovation. The position offers a clearly defined pathway toward doctoral or postdoctoral qualification, as well as structured opportunities for career development in academia and industry.

Application Documents and Procedure

Applications must be submitted in complete form and include all required formal documents, in particular:

a curriculum vitae,
certificates and transcripts of academic degrees,
proof of language proficiency.

Applicants must further submit a cover letter in which they explicitly address and document compliance with the six eligibility criteria ("this makes you a good fit").

Incomplete applications or applications not meeting the eligibility criteria may not be considered.

After submission, the application process is multi-staged and will include analytical tasks as part of the selection procedure.

THESE ARE YOUR TASKS:
design, implement, and evaluate deep neural, probabilistic, and hybrid symbolic-learning models for real-time sensor fusion, anomaly detection, and latent state estimation in dynamic bioreactor environments
adapt and extend large language models (LLMs) and symbolic reasoning frameworks to interpret domain-specific process logs, develop predictive diagnostics, and support explainable and trustworthy decision-making pipelines for bioprocess engineers
develop multi-modal bioinformatics pipelines for temporal single-cell analysis based on longitudinal pattern mining, geometric and graph deep learning
investigate reinforcement learning, simulator-based inference, and adaptive control strategies for feedback control and trajectory tracking in bioprocesses characterized by high uncertainty and complex nonlinear dynamics
collaborate closely with industrial partners and domain experts to curate multimodal process data, design and execute validation experiments, perform simulation studies, and iteratively refine models for deployment in real-world bioprocess settings
THIS MAKES YOU A GOOD FIT:
academic qualifications:
completed bachelor's and master’s degree in Computer Science, or a 5-year-diploma in Computer Science (or a comparative course of study with a predominantly computer science curriculum)
the qualifying degree must have been completed with good results (2.0 or better according to the German grading system; corresponding to a CGPA of approximately 85% or higher)
degree must have been awarded by a higher education institution (recognized as H+ according to the German Anabin system)
completed doctoral degree in Computer Science awarded with a final grade of magna cum laude or higher is prefered
language proficiency: applicants must meet either of the following language profiles:
proficiency in written and spoken English at level C1 CEFR (e.g. IELTS overall score ≥ 7.0, or TOEFL ≥ 94); or
native-level proficiency in German and demonstrated English language skill
research experience and analytical skillset:
documented experience in artificial intelligence, statistical / probabilistic modeling and/or data analysis is required
demonstrated analytical skillset, ability to solve novel and complex tasks independently and present results coherently
organizational and time-management skills: demonstrated ability to work independently and reliably under time constraints and to meet deadlines
communication skills: proven ability to communicate scientific results effectively, both orally and in written form
interdisciplinary competence: demonstrated ability to work collaboratively in interdisciplinary research environments
WE AS AN EMPLOYER:

Equal opportunities are important to us. We welcome applications from suitable severely disabled people or people from traditionally underrepresented groups. We aim to increase the proportion of women in research and teaching and therefore encourage suitably qualified women to apply. We welcome applications from people of other nationalities or with a migration background.

WE OFFER YOU:
FURTHER INFORMATION:

We will determine the experience level individually, taking into account your previous professional experience.

Incomplete application documents may not be considered in the further course of the selection process.

Unfortunately, we can not cover application and travel costs.

Become part of the team

The Hybrid Methods in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning group is part of the Institute for Visual & Analytic Computing at the Faculty of Computer Science and Electrical Engineering at the University of Rostock. Its research focuses on the integration of symbolic, probabilistic, and neural approaches in AI & ML. The application domains include AI-supported digital twins, intelligent environments, situation-aware assistance, and multimodal diagnostics. The methodological focus is on sequential state estimation using hybrid Bayesian and neuro-symbolic models, as well as the integration of deep learning with symbolic background knowledge. The positions are co-supervised by Hessian.AI group leader Martin Becker (Marburg University) focusing on knowledge-centric AI and biomedical ML with a strong international network.

We look forward to receiving your application!

The University of Rostock offers a diverse, varied and challenging position in a tradition-conscious, yet innovative, modern and family-friendly university in a lively city by the sea.

At the Faculty of Computer Science and Electrical Engineering, Institute for Visual and Analytic Computing, subject to allocation of funds, we are filling the following position at the earliest possible date on a temporary basis for the duration of the project Nexcell ending on 31.12.2028:

Research Assistant - Doctoral or Postdoctoral (m/f/d) - AI

Start date at the earliest possible date

Working hours full-time with 40 hours

Remuneration pay group 13 TV-L

Location Rostock

Tender number P 65/2026

Limitation limited until 31.12.2028

Application time 2026-06-15

Please do not hesitate to contact us for further information:

HR department:
Pia-Lucy Dahl
Phone number: 0381/498-1291
E-mail:

Department:
Prof. Dr.-Ing. Thomas Kirste
Phone number: 0381/498-7510
E-mail:

NEXCELL is a major multi-million-€ collaborative research initiative that brings together leading industrial and academic partners to create a groundbreaking point-of-care platform for next-generation cell and gene therapies. The vision is to enable personalized cancer treatments to be manufactured directly at the clinical site – making life-saving therapies more accessible and scalable. Within this project, the University of Rostock serves as the AI technology provider, developing a probabilistic digital twin of the NEXCELL bioreactor system. This digital twin will combine Bayesian AI, deep learning, symbolic reasoning, and hybrid modeling techniques to intelligently monitor and predict both technical and biological processes. By addressing challenges such as uncertainty quantification, multimodal sensor fusion, anomaly detection, and robust state estimation, our research will push the frontiers of AI in complex, safety-critical, and data-sparse domains. For motivated AI researchers, NEXCELL offers a unique opportunity to conduct fundamental research at the interface of cutting-edge machine learning and real-world bioprocess applications, with the potential for high-impact publications, open-source contributions, and direct collaboration with a market leader in bioreactor technology.

We invite an outstanding doctoral or postdoctoral researcher to join our institute in collaboration with a global industrial leader in bioreactor technology. The successful candidate will contribute to a large-scale, interdisciplinary research project focused on the development and deployment of advanced artificial intelligence methods for system monitoring, state estimation, and decision support in next-generation bioreactor platforms.

The project addresses theoretical and applied challenges at the intersection of machine learning, probabilistic modeling, symbolic AI, single cell analysis, and bioprocess engineering, with strong relevance to both academic research and industrial innovation. The position offers a clearly defined pathway toward doctoral or postdoctoral qualification, as well as structured opportunities for career development in academia and industry.

Application Documents and Procedure

Applications must be submitted in complete form and include all required formal documents, in particular:

  • a curriculum vitae,
  • certificates and transcripts of academic degrees,
  • proof of language proficiency.

Applicants must further submit a cover letter in which they explicitly address and document compliance with the six eligibility criteria ("this makes you a good fit").

Incomplete applications or applications not meeting the eligibility criteria may not be considered.

After submission, the application process is multi-staged and will include analytical tasks as part of the selection procedure.

THESE ARE YOUR TASKS:

  • design, implement, and evaluate deep neural, probabilistic, and hybrid symbolic-learning models for real-time sensor fusion, anomaly detection, and latent state estimation in dynamic bioreactor environments
  • adapt and extend large language models (LLMs) and symbolic reasoning frameworks to interpret domain-specific process logs, develop predictive diagnostics, and support explainable and trustworthy decision-making pipelines for bioprocess engineers
  • develop multi-modal bioinformatics pipelines for temporal single-cell analysis based on longitudinal pattern mining, geometric and graph deep learning
  • investigate reinforcement learning, simulator-based inference, and adaptive control strategies for feedback control and trajectory tracking in bioprocesses characterized by high uncertainty and complex nonlinear dynamics
  • collaborate closely with industrial partners and domain experts to curate multimodal process data, design and execute validation experiments, perform simulation studies, and iteratively refine models for deployment in real-world bioprocess settings

THIS MAKES YOU A GOOD FIT:

  • academic qualifications:
  • completed bachelor's and master’s degree in Computer Science, or a 5-year-diploma in Computer Science (or a comparative course of study with a predominantly computer science curriculum)
  • the qualifying degree must have been completed with good results (2.0 or better according to the German grading system; corresponding to a CGPA of approximately 85% or higher)
  • degree must have been awarded by a higher education institution (recognized as H+ according to the German Anabin system)
  • completed doctoral degree in Computer Science awarded with a final grade of magna cum laude or higher is prefered
  • language proficiency: applicants must meet either of the following language profiles:
  • proficiency in written and spoken English at level C1 CEFR (e.g. IELTS overall score 7.0, or TOEFL 94); or
  • native-level proficiency in German and demonstrated English language skill
  • research experience and analytical skillset:
  • documented experience in artificial intelligence, statistical / probabilistic modeling and/or data analysis is required
  • demonstrated analytical skillset, ability to solve novel and complex tasks independently and present results coherently
  • organizational and time-management skills: demonstrated ability to work independently and reliably under time constraints and to meet deadlines
  • communication skills: proven ability to communicate scientific results effectively, both orally and in written form
  • interdisciplinary competence: demonstrated ability to work collaboratively in interdisciplinary research environments

WE AS AN EMPLOYER:

Equal opportunities are important to us. We welcome applications from suitable severely disabled people or people from traditionally underrepresented groups. We aim to increase the proportion of women in research and teaching and therefore encourage suitably qualified women to apply. We welcome applications from people of other nationalities or with a migration background.

WE OFFER YOU:

We will determine the experience level individually, taking into account your previous professional experience.

If you would like to work part-time in this position, this is possible subject to the requirements of the position.

Job ID: 69826
(Senior) Manager Contract Risk Management (m/f/d)

Mannheim, BW, DE, 68163

Partial Remote (Hybrid)

Bilfinger is an international industrial services provider. The aim of the Group's activities is to increase the efficiency and sustainability of customers in the process industry and to establish itself as the number one partner in the market for this purpose. Bilfinger’s comprehensive portfolio covers the entire value chain from consulting, engineering, manufacturing, assembly, maintenance and plant expansion to turnarounds and digital applications.

The company delivers its services in two service lines: Engineering & Maintenance and Technologies. Bilfinger is primarily active in Europe, North America and the Middle East.
Process industry customers come from sectors that include energy, chemicals & petrochemicals, pharma & biopharma and oil & gas. With over 30,000 employees, Bilfinger upholds the highest standards of safety and quality and generated revenue of more than €5 billion in financial year 2024. To achieve its goals, Bilfinger has identified two strategic levers: positioning itself as a leader in increasing efficiency and sustainability and driving operational excellence to improve organizational performance.

Location: Mannheim or Frankfurt am Main

As a (Senior) Manager Contract Risk Management (m/f/d), you are responsible, within the framework of an overarching risk management system, for assessing and, where necessary, mitigating technical and commercial risks of projects and service/framework contracts that exceed defined threshold values — both during the bid and negotiation phases and throughout the execution phase of awarded contracts. You support the Stage‑Gate process, conduct risk analyses, and collaborate closely with relevant Corporate Functions and operational units to ensure effective project oversight and governance.

Your main tasks:

Governance and Reporting

Definition and maintenance of risk classification standards and Group‑wide minimum requirements for bids in collaboration with Group Legal and Compliance
Ensuring compliance with and governance of the Stage‑Gate process, including reporting to segment leadership or the Executive Board
Contribution to the further development of project standards and tools (including Procon database)
Preparation of quarterly reports on tenders and contract execution status
Participation in due‑diligence reviews and preparation of ad‑hoc risk reports for M&A processes

Involvement and Participation in the bid phase

Early assessment of contractual risks and opportunities for bids exceeding defined thresholds
Independent review of bids in accordance with the Group Standards and reporting to the responsible management levels

Continuous Project Controlling

Monitoring contract execution for project, service, and framework agreements with a focus on cost, schedule, quality, and risk
Early identification of deviations and proactive communication with relevant stakeholders
Support in the development and implementation of risk mitigation measures
Collaborate closely with Group Controlling and Project Audit to ensure data quality and address audit recommendations early

Our requirements:

Degree in Industrial Engineering, Business Administration, or a comparable field
Several years of experience in project management, project controlling, and auditing
Strong expertise in technical and commercial project management, including project and contract controlling, as well as a good knowledge of project‑related risks and opportunities with the ability to provide precise assessments and recommendations
Desirable certifications: Project Management (e.g., PMI) or Auditing (e.g., CIA, CISA)
SAP experience is an advantage
Excellent analytical skills paired with a structured and solution-oriented working style
Proven ability to work effectively in interdisciplinary and international teams
Business‑fluent German and English skills, both written and spoken
Flexibility and willingness to travel

We offer:

A responsible and varied range of tasks.
An open and motivated team that warmly welcomes you.
Plenty of room for your own ideas and creative solutions.
Challenging projects in an international environment with diverse development opportunities.
Personal development opportunities in various areas as well as professional advancements.
Flexible working hours within a flexitime system and the possibility of mobile working.
Structured onboarding for a pleasant and professional start.
Competitive compensation and additional benefits:
Vacation and Christmas bonuses.
30 days of vacation per calendar year
Entitlement to capital-forming benefits
Company pension scheme through direct insurance
Corporate benefits: Discounts with well-known manufacturers and brands
Free parking, job ticket/Germany ticket and access to EGYM Wellpass fitness studios
Free beverages at the locations
Modern and ergonomic workstations

For further information please contact Laura Schulz laura.schulz@bilfinger.com.

Bilfinger SE

Finance & Controlling

Permanent

Professional

Rohde & Schwarz is a global technology group striving for a safer and connected world. With
its Test & Measurement, Technology Systems and Networks & Cybersecurity Divisions, the
company creates tomorrow's innovations today. The company's leading-edge products and
solutions empower industrial, regulatory and military customers to attain technological and
digital sovereignty.

The privately owned company is known for stability and resilience. Independence is at the
core of its entrepreneurial identity. The company finances its growth with its own resources.
Because the company does not have to think in terms of quarterly results, it can plan sustainably for the long term. The high vertical integration of Rohde & Schwarz makes the
company a reliable, trustworthy and relevant partner for its customers. 

On June 30, 2025, Rohde & Schwarz had more than 15,000 employees worldwide. The group generated net revenue of EUR 3.16 billion in the 2024/2025 fiscal year (July to June).

Job Description:

In order to support our global logistics network and mission-critical operations, Airbus Defence and Space is looking for a Transport Project Manager (d/f/m)

Are you motivated to develop complex transport solutions and drive innovation within a high-stakes environment? We are looking for a strategic thinker to lead our national and international military transport projects. You will join a dedicated logistics team where precision meets agility, ensuring that our high-tech assets reach their destination safely, legally, and on time.

This role is also available with reduced weekly hours and an adapted scope of tasks.

Your location

Located about an hour’s drive north of Munich, Manching is an up-and-coming market town that offers a wide range of leisure and cultural activities. Here, you can enjoy the quality of life in the countryside while the pleasures of near-by cities are still within easy re ach.

Your benefits

Financial: Attractive salary, special payments, and employee stock options.

Balance: 30 days paid vacation, extra days off for special occasions, and flexible working models (job sharing, part-time, individual timeouts).

Growth: Excellent upskilling opportunities and international development prospects within the Airbus Group.

Health & Family: On-site medical services, canteen/cafeteria, and a kindergarten close to the site.

Extras: Employer-funded pension, discounted car leasing, insurance subsidies, and public transport support.

Culture: A diverse environment with over 140 nationalities where every voice is heard.

Your tasks and responsibilities

Strategic Planning: Develop comprehensive concepts for national and international military transports while ensuring 100% compliance with legal and safety regulations.

End-to-End Project Lead: Take full responsibility for the planning, organization, and execution of inbound and outbound transport projects—from the initial tender phase to final delivery—balancing time, cost, and quality.

Regulatory Compliance: Navigate complex export control, military regulations, and international trade laws to ensure seamless cross-border movements.

Risk & Safety Management: Conduct detailed risk analyses and develop robust safety concepts; create and monitor budgets while maintaining strict cost control.

Stakeholder Management: Collaborate with internal departments, government authorities, and external partners to streamline processes and provide regular status reporting to senior management.

Continuous Improvement: Analyze existing processes to identify synergies and optimization potential, driving efficiency and quality across the logistics chain.

Desired skills and qualifications

Education: A completed degree in Logistics, Transport Management, Business Administration, or a comparable qualification.

Professional Experience: Several years of expertise in project management within transport and logistics, ideally with a focus on military environments or sensitive cargo.

Regulatory Mastery: Deep familiarity with international transport regulations (ADR, IMDG, IATA-DGR) and a clear understanding of export control frameworks (e.g., ITAR, EAR, EU Dual-Use, KrWaffKontrG).

Communication: Excellent command of both English and German (written and spoken); additional languages are a valued asset in our international team.

Soft Skills: High sense of responsibility, resilience under pressure, and a collaborative team spirit combined with strong analytical and organizational capabilities.

Not a 100% match? No worries! Airbus supports your personal growth with customized development solutions.

Take your career to a new level and apply online now!

This job requires an awareness of any potential compliance risks and a commitment to act with integrity, as the foundation for the Company’s success, reputation and sustainable growth.

  • Extras: Employer-funded pension, discounted car leasing, insurance subsidies, and public transport support.

  • Culture: A diverse environment with over 140 nationalities where every voice is heard.

Your tasks and responsibilities

  • Strategic Planning: Develop comprehensive concepts for national and international military transports while ensuring 100% compliance with legal and safety regulations.

  • End-to-End Project Lead: Take full responsibility for the planning, organization, and execution of inbound and outbound transport projects—from the initial tender phase to final delivery—balancing time, cost, and quality.

  • Regulatory Compliance: Navigate complex export control, military regulations, and international trade laws to ensure seamless cross-border movements.

  • Risk & Safety Management: Conduct detailed risk analyses and develop robust safety concepts; create and monitor budgets while maintaining strict cost control.

  • Stakeholder Management: Collaborate with internal departments, government authorities, and external partners to streamline processes and provide regular status reporting to senior management.

  • Continuous Improvement: Analyze existing processes to identify synergies and optimization potential, driving efficiency and quality across the logistics chain.

Desired skills and qualifications

  • Education: A completed degree in Logistics, Transport Management, Business Administration, or a comparable qualification.

  • Professional Experience: Several years of expertise in project management within transport and logistics, ideally with a focus on military environments or sensitive cargo.

  • Regulatory Mastery: Deep familiarity with international transport regulations (ADR, IMDG, IATA-DGR) and a clear understanding of export control frameworks (e.g., ITAR, EAR, EU Dual-Use, KrWaffKontrG).

  • Communication: Excellent command of both English and German (written and spoken); additional languages are a valued asset in our international team.

  • Soft Skills: High sense of responsibility, resilience under pressure, and a collaborative team spirit combined with strong analytical and organizational capabilities.

Not a 100% match? No worries! Airbus supports your personal growth with customized development solutions.

Take your career to a new level and apply online now!

This job requires an awareness of any potential compliance risks and a commitment to act with integrity, as the foundation for the Company’s success, reputation and sustainable growth.

companyInfo/companyDescription:
We are a global leader in veterinary care, dedicated to providing exceptional animal care. Our network of first-opinion practices, referral hospitals, and emergency care centres is focused on our collective goal of healthy animals and happy owners.

For our team members, we offer clinical independence, a genuine commitment to your well-being, incredible support, and defined opportunities to develop; whatever the stage of your career, find your next step with IVC Evidensia.

Be part of our global network of skilled veterinary professionals and discover our support for flexible working and our dedication to inclusivity, as we build the world’s best veterinary group for our patients and our people.
description:
IVC Evidensia is one of the world’s leading veterinary care providers, bringing together a global community of more than 41,000 dedicated professionals across 20 countries. We care for over 8.5 million animals each year, spanning first‑opinion clinics, state‑of‑the‑art referral hospitals, farm and equine practices, and a wide range of supporting services. Our organization is built on a simple but powerful purpose: healthy animals and happy owners, delivered through industry‑leading clinical excellence and local empowerment.

As a fast‑growing international organization, we combine the strength of a global platform with the agility and local decision‑making that allow our businesses to thrive in their markets.

To strengthen our rapidly growing team, we are looking for a

Indirect Procurement Manager DACH (m/f/d)

This role is ideally based in Munich, home of our DACH headquarters, but we also welcome applicants based remotely anywhere in Germany. You will join a truly international environment with strong cross‑border collaboration and the ability to influence at both regional and global level.

You will report to the Global Indirect Procurement Director, with a planned transition to a future Head of Procurement Central Europe as we continue to evolve our operating model.

Key Responsibilities

Lead end-to-end procurement projects across indirect spend categories in the DACH region

Deliver measurable cost savings and EBITDA improvements

Develop and execute procurement strategies aligned with group-wide objectives

Run structured RFQs, tenders, and supplier negotiations

Conduct data-driven spend and cost analyses to identify optimization opportunities

Build and maintain strong, strategic supplier relationships

Prepare clear business cases and present recommendations to regional management

Ensure structured handover of contracts and savings initiatives to Operations and Finance after signature

Collaborate closely with global procurement colleagues and functional stakeholders

Your Profile

Master’s degree in Business Administration, Engineering, Economics, or a comparable field

5+ years of relevant experience in procurement, consulting, or a commercial role

Solid experience managing RFQs, tenders, and negotiations independently

Strong analytical mindset with advanced Excel skills

Proven ability to prioritize effectively and focus on value creation

Confident communicator, able to present complex topics clearly and concisely

Fluency in German and English

Comfortable working in an international, matrix-driven organization

Hands-on, pragmatic, and results-oriented working style

Nice to Have

Experience in a fast-growing or international organization

Exposure to working closely with senior management or executive stakeholders

Familiarity with indirect spend categories such as IT, facilities, professional services, or marketing

Have we sparked your interest?

Then we look forward to receiving your application, including details of your salary expectations and availability!

We look forward to getting to know you personally.

Zentrales Team

IVC Evidensia DACH

companyInfo/companyDescription:
Mensch, Stahl und Technologie – das sind wir. Bei uns steht der Mensch im Mittelpunkt: Denn erst durch unsere Mitarbeiterinnen und Mitarbeiter eröffnet sich der Blick auf die Vielfalt unserer Produkte und Dienstleistungen. Ob Spezialstähle für den automobilen Leichtbau, Rohre für Kraftwerke und Pipelines, Getränkeabfüllanlagen oder Spritzgießmaschinen – unser Spektrum ist so breit wie spannend.

Bei uns trifft Tradition auf Innovation und Pioniergeist. Wir schaffen ein Umfeld, in dem Sie Ihre Fähigkeiten voll entfalten und kontinuierlich weiterentwickeln können. Wir fördern Eigenverantwortung und einen offenen Austausch, so entstehen die besten Ideen und Lösungen.
Anerkennung, faire und leistungsorientierte Vergütung sowie umfangreiche Qualifizierungsangebote sind für uns selbstverständlich. Wir glauben an gemeinsamen Erfolg: Jeder Beitrag zählt, denn unser Erfolg ist immer der Erfolg jedes Einzelnen.

Wir wissen, wer wir sind, und wir sind stolz auf unser Handeln. Vielfalt ist unsere Stärke – in Produkten, Menschen und Ideen. Mit unserem Leitbild „YOUNITED – Gemeinsam mehr erreichen“ leben wir unsere Werte jeden Tag.

Sei Teil von etwas Großem – gestalte mit uns die Zukunft, in der Menschen, Technik und Visionen zusammenkommen.
description:
Process Engineer Water Technology (m/f/d)

KHS is a subsidiary of Salzgitter AG. As one of the world's leading manufacturers of filling and packaging systems for beverages and liquid food we are a world-class player. Our customers have trusted in our passionate pioneering spirit and first-class technologies for over 150 years. However, we can only remain world class if we continue to find new employees who make just as high demands of themselves and the quality of their work as our customers make of us at KHS. Are you one of them?

We are currently seeking an Process Engineer Water Technology (m/f/d) to join our “Innoplant” department at our Bad Kreuznach site.

Ihre Aufgaben
Intensive customer consulting and design of industrial water and wastewater treatment plants for turnkey projects in the beverage industry, worldwide.
Analysis of customer requirements and local conditions of the respective region.
Conceptual preparation of internal and external tender documents.
Planning of treatment plants for all required process water and wastewater for turnkey projects using all standard market technologies.
Quotation preparation and project handling for water technology in the beverage industry.
Supplier selection and technical clarification for water and wastewater treatment plants.
Project execution for water plants in close cooperation with the Project Manager.
Ihr Profil
Technical training or degree (e.g., process engineering/mechanical engineering with a focus on water technology or comparable)
Several years of professional experience in technical sales or in the planning of water technology, ideally in the food or beverage industry
Excellent knowledge of MS Office and MS Project
Business-fluent English; knowledge of another foreign language is desirable
Goal-oriented and structured approach to work
Ability to work in a team, assertiveness, and professional demeanor
Efficient time management, flexibility to adapt to project requirements, strong customer focus, and willingness to travel internationally
Einsatzort: Bad Kreuznach ǀ Rheinland-Pfalz
Karte anzeigen

KHS GmbH

Planiger Straße 139 - 147, 55543 Bad Kreuznach, Deutschland

companyInfo/companyDescription: Exyte is a global leader in the design, engineering, and delivery of ultra-clean and sustainable facilities for high-tech industries. With cutting-edge expertise developed over more than a century, the company serves clients in the sophisticated markets of semiconductors, battery cells, pharmaceuticals, biotechnology, and data centers. Exyte offers a full range of services from consulting to managing the implementation of turnkey solutions with the highest standards in safety and quality to its customers worldwide. Exyte creates a better future by enabling key industries to enhance the quality of modern life. In 2022, the company generated sales of €7.4 billion with around 9,000 employees worldwide. www.exyte.net
description:
Discover your exciting role

Exyte are now hiring for a Expert Quantity Survey CSA (m/f/d), responsible for the preconstruction commercial management of CSA scope on Data Center projects in Germany.

Explore your tasks and responsibilities

• Analyze drawings, specifications, and tender documents to estimate quantities and costs
• Ensure effective coordination and timely handling of all tender related activities and responses
• Review proposals with designers and coordinate subcontractor enquiries
• Lead the procurement process for subcontract CSA work packages
• Subcontractor management
• Manage and monitor all commercial aspects of CSA packages across assigned projects
• Responsible for cost controlling and work closely with Project Managers regarding contract variations
• Support supply chain development and preferred supplier alignment for CSA work packages

Show your expertise

• Degree in Quantity Surveying, Engineering, or related area
• Several years relevant work experiences with a main contractor, ideally within Data Centers
• Excellent English language skills; good German languages skills are a plus
• Ability to work collaboratively in a dynamic, fast paced project environment
• Motivation and independent working style to push topics independently and persistently and to achieve high quality results
• Excellent negotiation and communication skills

What we offer you

• Are you looking for challenging projects? We offer you the opportunity to actively participate in customized, large-scale technological projects in engineering and construction.
• Do you appreciate working in a multidisciplinary environment? We are the right place for you.
• Your start at Exyte: Onboarding, on the job training and a variety of online and classroom trainings (e. g. language training German or English) offered via our extensive Learning Management System.
• Buddy-System: Several weeks of close familiarisation with your colleagues in the team.
• Keep fit: Strong body, strong mind. Exyte subsidises your fitness contract through Qualitrain at over 5,000 locations in Germany.
• You want to actively shape the future? At Exyte you will find the innovative and constantly growing environment you need for that. So, what are you waiting for? Become part of this change!

Contact:

You want to be part of the Exyte team? We look forward to receiving your application!
For further questions and information, please do not hesitate to contact Lisa Rakowsky via E-Mail at lisa.rakowsky@exyte.net.

Please note that we only consider applications submitted through our application portal. Applications sent via email will not be considered due to data protection regulations.

description:
Discover your exciting role

As a Senior Process Engineer Packaging (m/f/d) specializing in pharmaceutical and biotechnological process design, you will join a highly qualified team at our regional office in Bad Homburg, where knowledge sharing and collaborative work are essential. You will work in an international environment with open��minded, professional, and engaged colleagues, supported by a culture that promotes continuous improvement and development.

Explore your tasks and responsibilities

• Lead process planning for Packaging based on biotechnological active‑ingredient production and related technologies.
• Oversee all project phases with budget responsibility, from concept development through execution, installation supervision, commissioning, and qualification.
• Document customer requirements, support concept development, and present results to internal and external stakeholders.
• Define technical process requirements, including layout creation and process flow diagrams.
• Prepare technical tender documents and support equipment and IT system selection through commercial and technical bid evaluations.
• Act as the lead process engineering interface for interdisciplinary teams, including piping, IC, automation, and utilities engineering.
• Lead logistics process engineering teams as needed and participate in risk analyses such as HAZOP and FMEA.

Show your expertise

• Bachelor’s degree or higher in supply chain management, logistics, industrial engineering, computer science, business administration, biotechnology, pharmaceutical sciences, or a related field.
• Professional, supportive, and results‑driven leadership style within project structures, combined with strong IT affinity and deep understanding of logistics processes.
• Experience in logistics process management using transport, warehouse, and/or packaging IT systems such as SAP IM/WM, Navision, or similar tools.
• Strong analytical and communication skills, with enthusiasm for collaborating with diverse stakeholders in an international environment.
• Proactive, solution‑oriented mindset with high stress resilience, strong attention to detail, and a focused, structured working style; self‑driven and team‑oriented.
• Professional logistics engineering experience of 5+ years is considered an advantage.
• Fluent in German and English, both written and spoken.
• Willingness to travel up to 30%.

What we offer you

• Growth opportunities in an interesting and complex industry sector
• Bring in your ideas and proposal to shape the success of Process Engineer Logistics and Packaging at Exyte
• Your start at Exyte: Take part in a two-day onboarding event with new colleagues
• Buddy-System: Several weeks of close familiarization with your colleagues in the team
• Work-life balance: There is always the possibility of switching to hybrid working
• Catering: Free coffee specialities as well as water dispensers are accessible
• Keep fit: Exyte subsidises your fitness contract through EGYM Wellpass at over 5,000 locations in Germany

Contact:

You want to be part of the Exyte team? We look forward to receiving your application!
For further questions and information, please do not hesitate to contact Gerd Fleischmann via E-Mail at gerd.fleischmann@exyte.net.

Please note that we only consider applications submitted through our application portal. Applications sent via email will not be considered due to data protection regulations.

Are you ready to help shaping the construction industry and drive digital innovation? Do you share our passion for successful construction projects and digital infrastructure? Then we’d love to meet you! Join our team—we aim to lay the foundation for the digital future! In a fast-paced world where innovation and progress go hand in hand, we are looking for a committed

Time Scheduler / Work Preparation Engineer

Time Scheduler / Work Preparation Engineer

Your Responsibilities

During tender preparation for national or international building projects in the "High-Tech" sector (e.g. data centers), you will be responsible for the preparation of the construction schedule across all phases and milestones.
You will identify and assess construction time optimization potentials as well as opportunities and risks in the design and construction process.
You will transform tender schedules into reliable contractual baseline schedules, working schedules, or detailed trade-wise and specific schedules. You will analyze subcontractor delays and develop countermeasures for an on-time delivery.
During project execution, you will be a permanent member of the site team, maintaining these schedules with regular planned vs. actual comparisons.
You will integrate results from the weekly Last Planner meetings and monitor subcontractors concerning their schedule commitments and construction obligations.
In addition, you will implement and maintain a meaningful internal and external reporting system. Prepare progress reports, schedule analyses, presentations. Assist in claim/change order analysis using forensic scheduling.

Your Profile

You have successfully completed a degree in civil engineering or a comparable technical qualification.
You possess well-founded experience in work preparation and time scheduling on complex building projects, ideally in the construction of data centers or other advance tech projects ("Mission Critical Facilities").
You have good to excellent knowledge of Primavera, Astra Powerproject, and MS Project
High motivation, team and communication skills are second nature to you.
Good German and English language skills, both spoken and written, are essential.
Flexibility and willingness to travel throughout Germany and neighboring European countries mean an exciting challenge for you.

Your Benefits

Secure a permanent position in a globally operating company with exciting and future-proof projects
Take advantage of diverse training and development opportunities to advance your career
Enjoy an attractive salary and extensive social benefits
Achieve a healthy work-life balance with 30 days of vacation, plus 3 additional days and the option for remote work
Benefit from a company car and exclusive employee benefits, such as health management programs

Sounds interesting?

Do not hesitate and apply now via the online form. If you have any questions, Kathrin Zimmer (Tel.: +49 201 824 4244) will be very happy to answer any question you have.

Who we are

HOCHTIEF is one of the world's leading construction groups. Its focus is on complex infrastructure projects, many are based on concession models. HOCHTIEF additionally develops and operates real estate and facilities. With its global network HOCHTIEF is present in the world‘s major markets.

companyInfo/companyDescription: Exyte is a global leader in the design, engineering, and delivery of ultra-clean and sustainable facilities for high-tech industries. With cutting-edge expertise developed over more than a century, the company serves clients in the sophisticated markets of semiconductors, battery cells, pharmaceuticals, biotechnology, and data centers. Exyte offers a full range of services from consulting to managing the implementation of turnkey solutions with the highest standards in safety and quality to its customers worldwide. Exyte creates a better future by enabling key industries to enhance the quality of modern life. In 2022, the company generated sales of €7.4 billion with around 9,000 employees worldwide. www.exyte.net
description:
Location: Bad Homburg, Germany
Category: Procurement
Job ID: 23089
Permanent
Exyte Germany GmbH (1001)
Discover your exciting role

As part of the core project team, you will manage and lead all purchasing and subcontracting activities of a project: from tender phase through execution to closing. You can make the difference. The results of your work have a direct impact on Exyte’s business success. Join us and be part of a multicultural, international growing team.

Explore your tasks and responsibilities
Define the procurement strategy and create and maintain the project procurement plan across all procurement packages
Provide functional leadership and guidance to all strategic buyers and subcontracting managers within a specific project
Ensure overall supply chain performance, timely delivery of materials, and the provision of services
Manage timely payment of supplier invoices and oversee claim management
Act as the central contact person for all purchasing functions and roles, representing them in the core project team
Show your expertise
Completed degree in Business Administration, Engineering, or a related field
Several years’ experience in procurement and project management in large-scale projects
Proven experience in data centers, high-tech, or comparable complex construction environments
Experience in leading cross-functional teams and strong communication skills
Proficient in both English and German, spoken and written
Flexibility and willingness to travel nationally, with on-site work depending on the project phase
Decisiveness, openness to change, strong interpersonal skills, and a solution-oriented working style
What we offer you
Are you looking for challenging projects? We offer you the opportunity to actively participate in customized, large-scale technological projects in engineering and construction
Do you appreciate working in a multidisciplinary environment? We are the right place for you
Your start at Exyte: Onboarding, on the job training and a variety of online and classroom trainings (e. g. language training German or English) offered via our extensive Learning Management System
Buddy-System: Several weeks of close familiarisation with your colleagues in the team
Keep fit: Strong body, strong mind. Exyte subsidises your fitness contract through Qualitrain at over 5,000 locations in Germany
You want to actively shape the future? At Exyte you will find the innovative and constantly growing environment you need for that. So, what are you waiting for? Become part of this change!

Contact:

You want to be part of the Exyte team? We look forward to receiving your application!
For further questions and information, please do not hesitate to contact Lisa Rakowsky via E-Mail at lisa.rakowsky@exyte.net.

Please note that we only consider applications submitted through our application portal. Applications sent via email will not be considered due to data protection regulations.

description:
FÜLLE MIT UNS DIE KÜHLSCHRÄNKE EUROPAS – WILLKOMMEN IN DER FOODLOGISTIK!
Das sind die Aufgaben
Analyse von Ausschreibungs- und Kundenanforderungen sowie Entwicklung maßgeschneiderter Warehouse-, Prozess- und Logistikkonzepte für die Kontraktlogistik – von der ersten Idee bis zum finalen Lösungsdesign
Erstellung detaillierter Angebots- und Kundenkalkulationen (Business Cases) unter Einbeziehung von Personal-, Flächen-, Investitions-, IT-, Automatisierungs- und Betriebskosten
Durchführung von Nachkalkulationen und Wirtschaftlichkeitsanalysen für Bestandskunden sowie Identifikation von Optimierungs- und Skalierungspotenzialen in bestehenden Logistikkonzepten
Enge Zusammenarbeit mit Vertrieb, operativen Standorten, IT, Engineering und internationalen Einheiten zur Sicherstellung umsetzbarer, wettbewerbsfähiger und skalierbarer Lösungen
Fachliche Unterstützung des Vertriebs bei Kundenpräsentationen, Workshops und Vertragsverhandlungen
Sicherstellung, Anwendung und kontinuierliche Weiterentwicklung von Kalkulationsstandards, Tools und Methoden zur Steigerung von Qualität, Transparenz und Effizienz im Angebots- und Solutions-Design-Prozess
Weiterentwicklung von Standards, Tools und Methoden im Bereich Solution Design und Kalkulation
Was uns überzeugt
Abgeschlossene kaufmännische Berufsausbildung als Kaufmann/-frau Spedition und Logistikdienstleistung oder ein abgeschlossenes Studium, beispielsweise im Bereich BWL, idealerweise mit Schwerpunkt Logistik
Mehrjährige Berufserfahrung als Logistikplaner, Solution Designer, Tender Manager oder Supply Chain Engineer
Sehr gutes Verständnis logistischer Prozesse sowie Erfahrung in der Entwicklung von Warehouse- und Logistikkonzepten
Analytisches Denkvermögen sowie ein hohes Zahlenverständnis gepaart mit einer strukturierten und sorgfältigen Arbeitsweise
Kommunikationsstärke und sicheres Auftreten im Austausch mit internen und externen Stakeholdern, auch in anspruchsvollen Situationen
Eigenverantwortliches Arbeiten, hohe Verbindlichkeit und die Motivation, Prozesse aktiv mitzugestalten und weiterzuentwickeln
Verhandlungssichere Deutsch- und gute Englischkenntnisse
Was wir einbringen
Einen krisensicheren Arbeitsplatz in einer systemrelevanten Branche
Eine attraktive Vergütung und 30 Tage Urlaub
Gute Vereinbarkeit von Familie und Beruf durch flexible Arbeitszeiten und die Möglichkeit des mobilen Arbeitens
Unterstützung und Förderung der individuellen Karriereentwicklung
Über 100 Schulungsangebote durch unseren Nagel Online Campus
Mitarbeitervergünstigungen wie Corporate Benefits oder Jobrad
Bezuschusstes Essensangebot in unserer betriebseigenen Kantine
Ein leistungsstarkes, angenehmes Arbeitsumfeld und ein tolles Team

companyInfo/companyDescription:
Zimmer Biomet is a global medical technology leader with a comprehensive portfolio designed to maximize mobility and improve health. We seamlessly transform the patient experience through our innovative products and suite of integrated digital and robotic technologies that leverage data, data analytics and artificial intelligence.

With 90+ years of trusted leadership and proven expertise, Zimmer Biomet is positioned to deliver the highest quality solutions to patients and providers. Our legacy continues to come to life today through our progressive culture of evolution and innovation.
description:
At Zimmer Biomet, we believe in pushing the boundaries of innovation and driving our mission forward. As a global medical technology leader for nearly 100 years, a patient’s mobility is enhanced by a Zimmer Biomet product or technology every 8 seconds.

As a Zimmer Biomet team member, you will share in our commitment to providing mobility and renewed life to people around the world. To support our talent team, we focus on development opportunities, robust employee resource groups (ERGs), a flexible working environment, location specific competitive total rewards, wellness incentives and a culture of recognition and performance awards. We are committed to creating an environment where every team member feels included, respected, empowered and recognised.

What You Can Expect

This role is critical in ZB EMEA’s new operating model consisting of 3 BU’s (RDTS, SET & EM).

The EMEA Pricing Sr Director designs and delivers the EMEA pricing strategy, pricing governance, and leads the price setting, price getting and price netting processes across all business segments.

The Pricing Director leads a team of cluster pricing managers and their pricing & operations teams to ensure price increase programs are implemented successfully margin erosion is minimized and value capture is optimized.

The pricing team uses technology (Vendavo, Power BI, SAP) to improve price performance and maximize revenue growth and profitability within the region.

The Pricing Director will work closely with the segment leaders in defining the pricing strategy as well as with the cluster and country commercial leads and with Finance to implement it.

How You'll Create Impact

Set up and drive execution of EMEA pricing policy, governance framework and process.

Support BU marketing teams in developing EMEA pricing strategies, guidelines such as floor prices & margins per brand or SKU for new launches and key established brands
Deploy price reduction approval matrices (PRAMs) across all countries in EMEA
Identify sources of Margin leakage and pockets of opportunity to increase margins at all levels
Participate or delegate in DAAC approval process and review distributor list prices

The Pricing Director chairs the EMEA pricing committee and leads the process to prepare, recommend, document and track decisions of this committee.

Lead preparation, decision making, implementation & tracking of EMEA pricing committee
Set up a standardized EMEA pricing approval process for large and cross-border deals
Develop a process to manage cross-border pricing negotiations impacting multiple countries and BU’s

The Pricing Director drives the execution of insights derived from the Analytics CoE.

Design, build and deploy pricing technology including CPQ and analytics throughout EMEA
Review the process of adapting list prices periodically across countries and segments
Publishes a monthly EMEA ‘price report” to identify pricing trends, opportunities and challenges

Pricing Director ensures execution of price increase and margin expansion initiatives through direct reporting line of cluster Pricing Managers.

Works with country pricing/tendering/commercial teams to build EMEA-wide analytics, processes, tools and capabilities
Increase prices in line with corporate guidelines, cost inflation or other pressures to improve margins
Leads Pricing cluster Managers in conducting price sensitivity analyses across the ZB portfolio and actioning on price improvement opportunities
Work closely with Market Access to identify opportunities to improve pricing and reimbursement environment
What Makes You Stand Out

Ability to lead large multi-country, multi-cultural teams
Experience in implementing price management & price analytics technology across several countries
Expert knowledge of pricing models and techniques in medical devices in Europe
Expert knowledge of buying and tendering processes in medical devices in Europe
Strong analytical and quantitative modelling skills
Strong communication skills
Experience with change management and actioning change in a diverse organisation
Strong project management and cross-country collaboration skills
Strong written and verbal command of English + additional European language preferred
Strong work ethic, going beyond what is asked for and applying own critical thinking
Team player, high degree of flexibility and responsiveness to changing inputs
Can do mind-set, self-driven, never satisfied - continuous improvement mind-set
Your Background

Bachelor or higher. Business administration, finance or marketing preferred.
Minimum of 5 years of leading pricing teams in EMEA in medical devices.
Minimum of 10 years of hands-on pricing and tendering experience in EMEA in medical devices
Travel Expectations

Up to 30%

EOE/M/F/Vet/Disability
