companyInfo/companyDescription:
You may not know our name but we can guarantee you know our work. Eurofins is by your side every day, from the food you eat to the medicine you rely on. We work with the biggest companies in the world, making sure the products they supply are safe, their ingredients are authentic and the labelling is accurate.
We are now the global market leader in food, environment, pharmaceutical and cosmetic product testing, discovery pharmacology, forensics, advanced material sciences, and in agroscience Contract Research services.
description:
Company Description

Eurofins Genomics is globally known for its innovative and customized technologies in the life science industries and academic research. We are an international provider of genomic services around the core business lines next generation sequencing, genotyping, gene expression analysis, Sanger sequencing, oligonucleotide, and gene synthesis. Being passionate about our strong customer orientation, our service and quality standards is our highest priority. We are an entrepreneurial organization with a dynamic international management team, continuously seeking for new growth opportunities. We encourage our employees to take responsibility and offer personal leeway for shaping the future of the company.

Job Description

General conditions:
• Home office-based work, as well as occasional visits to production sites in Germany and Europe.
• Support during induction and training.
• The position is designed for 32 hours per week and is permanent.

You will be responsible for:

Increase sales in all genomics services (e.g., NGS, Sanger sequencing, oligos, gene synthesis, microarray, genotyping, custom projects) in the market segments of agrigomics, environmental & food genomics, biopharma genomics, and personal genomics. Increase sales growth through cross-selling and developing account potential.

Establishment and maintenance of a sales pipeline: identification of new projects and business opportunities.

Regular reporting (sales analysis, forecasting, competitive analysis) and intensive use of CRM, including ongoing database maintenance.

Involvement of technical experts for optimal project solutions.

Communication of market and customer feedback to internal teams.

Implementation of the sales strategy in coordination with the Regional Sales Manager.

Conducting product presentations and providing advice on genomics solutions.

Proactive identification, qualification, and conversion of new leads to expand the customer base via telephone, email, and team meetings.

Guiding new customers through the ordering process and ensuring customer satisfaction.

Processing public tenders – depending on the region.

Qualifications

Your profile:

Completed degree (BSc or higher) in a relevant scientific subject or at least 3 years of professional experience in customer contact in genomics.

In-depth knowledge of genomics applications and analysis methods.

Independent, goal-oriented approach to work – including when working from home.

Hunter mentality.

Strong communication, negotiation, and presentation skills.

Team player with high integrity and initiative.

Business fluent written and spoken German and English.

Personal Qualities:

High energy and passion for science and sales.

Enthusiasm for products and services—and the ability to convey that enthusiasm.

Empathy, strong interpersonal skills, and active listening.

Courage, initiative, risk-taking, and risk awareness.

Affinity for numbers, analytical thinking, and business acumen.

Cost awareness, action orientation, and strong work ethic.

Results-oriented, strategic thinking with an eye for detail.

Additional Information

We offer you:

A varied working day in a dynamic group of companies
30 days annual leave and various special leave days
Numerous social benefits, such as
Company pension scheme
Corporate benefits (discount codes for various providers)
Benefits in kind (€50 per month)
Health management (e.g. participation in health courses, regular eye examinations by our company doctor and much more)
Wide range of further training opportunities
Free coffee and tea & fresh fruit

Eurofins Scientific is an international life sciences company, providing a unique range of analytical testing services to clients across multiple industries, to make life and our environment safer, healthier and more sustainable. From the food you eat, to the water you drink, to the medicines you rely on, Eurofins works with the biggest companies in the world to ensure the products they supply are safe, their ingredients are authentic and labelling is accurate. Eurofins believes it is a global leader in food, environmental, pharmaceutical and cosmetics products testing and in agroscience CRO services. It is also one of the global independent market leaders in certain testing and laboratory services for genomics, discovery pharmacology, forensics, CDMO, advanced material sciences and in the support of clinical studies.

In over just 30 years, Eurofins has grown from one laboratory in Nantes, France to over 62,000 staff across a network of more than over 1000 independent companies in over 62 countries and operating more than 900 laboratories. Eurofins offers a portfolio of over 200,000 analytical methods to evaluate the safety, identity, composition, authenticity, origin, traceability and purity of biological substances and products, as well as providing innovative clinical diagnostic testing services, as one of the leading global emerging players in specialised clinical diagnostics testing.

In 2024, Eurofins generated total revenues of EUR € 6.515 billion, and has been among the best performing stocks in Europe over the past 20 years.

companyInfo/companyDescription: ABB ist ein führendes Technologieunternehmen in den Bereichen Elektrifizierung und Automation, das eine nachhaltigere und ressourceneffizientere Zukunft ermöglicht. Durch die Verbindung von technischer Expertise und Digitalisierung sorgt ABB dafür, dass Industrien hohe Leistungen erbringen und gleichzeitig effizienter, produktiver und nachhaltiger werden, um ihre Ziele zu übertreffen. Bei ABB nennen wir das «Engineered to Outrun». Das Unternehmen blickt auf eine über 140-jährige Geschichte zurück und beschäftigt mehr als 105.000 Mitarbeitende weltweit.
description:
At ABB, we help industries outrun - leaner and cleaner. Here, progress is an expectation - for you, your team, and the world. As a global market leader, we’ll give you what you need to make it happen. It won’t always be easy, growing takes grit. But at ABB, you’ll never run alone. Run what runs the world.

This role sits within ABB's Robotics business, a leading global robotics company. We're entering an exciting new chapter as we’ve announced the plan for SoftBank Group to acquire ABB Robotics. SoftBank is a globally recognized technology group and investor/operator focused on AI, robotics, and next-generation computing. By joining us now, you’ll be part of a pioneering team shaping the future of robotics—working alongside world-class experts in a fast-moving, innovation-driven environment.

This Position reports to:

Senior Legal Counsel

Key responsibilities

In this role, you will lead and coordinate contract and claim management activities across our Robotics & Discrete Automation operations in Friedberg. You will be a key contact when it comes to assessing contract risks, supporting tender processes, and protecting ABB’s commercial interests during project execution. With your strong legal and commercial understanding, you will work closely with the legal department and internal stakeholders to ensure compliance, mitigate risks, and resolve disputes where needed.

The work model for the role is: hybrid. #LI-Hybrid

This role is contributing to the Robotics & Discrete Automation Business Area in Friedberg (Germany). Main stakeholders include cross-functional departments at the Friedberg site and colleagues across the global ABB Robotics and Discrete Automation organization.

You will be mainly accountable for:

Identifying contract baselines and conducting risk assessments in line with internal guidelines and processes

Reviewing, negotiating, and managing selected contracts and non-disclosure agreements in close collaboration with the legal and business teams

Drafting and managing legal correspondence with customers and suppliers

Supporting the legal team in the resolution of disputes, claims, litigation, and arbitration

Protecting ABB’s contractual position throughout the project lifecycle, including change and deviation management

Ensuring compliance with the ABB Code of Conduct as well as global and local contract management standards and tools

You will join a collaborative and experienced team, where you will be able to contribute to your legal and commercial skills in a dynamic and international environment.

Qualifications for the role

Degree in Law, Business Law, or a comparable field with a solid understanding of economic and contractual frameworks

Practical experience in contract and claim management, ideally in an industrial or project-driven environment

Familiarity with contract law and risk assessment methodologies

Strong communication, negotiation, and problem-solving skills

Hands-on mentality with a structured and customer-oriented approach

Ability to collaborate effectively in cross-functional and international teams

Fluency in German and English, both written and spoken

Benefits

Retirement Plan

Flexible work practices

Employee Share Acquisition Plan

Learning & development offering

Interested in joining our team? We are looking forward to receiving your complete application through our online tool. In the case of equal suitability, people with severe disabilities and their equals are given preference.

ABB AG

+49 (0) 621 381 3500

Make your mark. This is where progress takes shape, industries evolve, and your presence is noted.
Run What Runs the World.

#ABBCareers

#RunwithABB

#Runwhatrunstheworld

We value people from different backgrounds. Could this be your story? Apply today or visit www.abb.com to read more about us and learn about the impact of our solutions across the globe.

companyInfo/companyDescription:
Giesecke+Devrient (G+D) is a global SecurityTech company headquartered in Munich, Germany. G+D makes the lives of billions of people more secure. The company shapes trust in the digital age, with built-in security technology in three segments: Digital Security, Financial Platforms and Currency Technology.

G+D was founded in 1852 and today has a workforce of more than 14,000 employees. In the fiscal year 2023, the company generated a turnover of 3 billion euros. G+D is represented by 123 subsidiaries and joint ventures in 40 countries. Further information: www.gi-de.com.
description:
G+D makes the lives of billions of people around the world more secure. We create trust in the digital age with integrated security technologies in three business areas: Digital Security, Financial Platforms and Currency Technology. We have been a reliable partner for our customers for over 170 years with our innovative solutions for SecurityTech! We are an international technology group and traditional family business with over 14,000 employees in 40 countries. Creating Confidence is our path to success. Trust is the basis of our co-operation within G+D.

The whole world trusts us when it comes to physical or digital currencies. We increase the security and efficiency of the cash cycle in collaboration with central banks and the entire currency industry. As the market leader in advanced currency management, would you like to join us in shaping the future of payments?

Your Tasks:
You take on bid management with a focus on project calculations and financial evaluations of tenders and proposals – particularly in the areas of cash center solutions and high-security printing.
You provide cross-functional support to the Bid and Contract Management team EU/LATAM/NAM.
You work closely and as an equal partner with our Sales, Technical Sales teams, the Contract Managers in Munich, as well as with our international subsidiaries.
You analyze customer-specific tender documents and develop a coherent bid strategy together with the international bid team.
You clarify project-related questions with the relevant departments – such as Export, Logistics, Insurance, Compliance, Legal, Procurement, Controlling, Tax, or Finance.
You prepare consolidated project costings, conduct risk assessments, and manage the internal approval process in line with company guidelines.
You identify opportunities, risks, and optimization potential within cost structures and derive concrete recommendations for action.
When required, you support the Sales team in commercial contract negotiations with customers.
You ensure accurate and audit-proof documentation and compliance with all formal and commercial requirements.
Once a contract is signed, you are responsible for the structured handover to the operational teams.
Your Profile:
You have completed a commercial apprenticeship and/or hold a university degree in Business Administration or STEM.
You have several years of professional experience in bid management, ideally combined with (sales) controlling or accounting.
You bring expert-level knowledge in cost and pricing calculations as well as in presenting commercial KPIs and financial metrics.
Risk assessments, scenario calculations, and cost structure analyses are among your strengths and motivate you.
You are capable of analyzing complex specifications and tender documents and translating them into accurate calculation models.
You have experience working with international standards and public procurement processes.
You possess basic contract knowledge, including in software-related environments.
Flexibility, analytical thinking, initiative, and a solution-oriented, structured working style distinguish you – complemented by a healthy degree of pragmatism.
You are fluent in German and English; an additional foreign language is an advantage.
You are willing to occasionally travel internationally to support customer negotiations together with Sales and/or Technical teams.
You are proficient in MS Office, especially Excel (including pivot tables).

A look behind the scenes
Contact
Hannah Distler

Are you ready to help shape the digital future? Is safety your highest value when managing complex projects? Join our team and contribute to building the foundation for digital transformation! In a fast-paced world where innovation and progress go hand in hand, we are looking for a committed

Project Manager (m/f/d) – Building Construction / High Tech Projects
Your Responsibilities

Leading a project team in future market construction projects such as data centers, battery factories, or other mission-critical facilities in Germany or the neighboring European countries.
Engage in acquisition activities, lead tender or Preconstruction efforts and ensure successful project win
Full responsibility for the successful project delivery including responsibility for health and safety, and all technical, financial, qualitative, scheduling, organizational, and contractual aspects
Full accountability for revenue, costs and positive project results
Holistic project management, including coordination and oversight of all services provided by internal and external project stakeholders

Your Profile

Successfully completed degree in Civil Engineering or Mechanical / Electrical Engineering
Several years of hands-on experience in the construction of data centers or similar advanced technology facilities with high MEP content
Extensive experience in implementing international health and safety standards in German / international projects, with a strong commitment to the “Safety First” principle
Strong leadership skills, motivation, and dedication
Excellent teamwork and communication skills
Very good German and English language skills, both spoken and written
Flexibility and willingness to travel for project assignments within Germany and Europe

Your Benefits

Unbefristete Festanstellung in einem global agierenden Unternehmen mit zukunftssicheren Projekten
 Verantwortungsvolle Führungsrolle in einem internationalen Umfeld
Vielfältige Weiterbildungs- und Entwicklungsmöglichkeiten, um Ihre Karriere voranzutreiben.
Attraktive Vergütung und umfangreiche Sozialleistungen

Dienstwagen und weitere exklusive Mitarbeiterangebote, z.B. im Gesundheitsmanagement

Do not hesitate and apply now via the online form. If you have any questions, Kathrin Zimmer (Tel.: +49 201 824-4244) will be very happy to answer any questions you have.

HOCHTIEF is one of the world's leading construction groups. Its focus is on complex infrastructure projects, many are based on concession models. HOCHTIEF additionally develops and operates real estate and facilities. With its global network HOCHTIEF is present in the world‘s major markets.

companyInfo/companyDescription:
Mehr als 150 Jahre inspirierende Technologie für Generationen

Voith ist ein weltweit agierender Technologiekonzern. Mit seinem breiten Portfolio aus Anlagen, Produkten, Serviceleistungen und digitalen Anwendungen setzt Voith Maßstäbe in den Märkten Energie, Öl & Gas, Papier, Rohstoffe und Transport & Automotive. Gegründet 1867 ist Voith heute mit mehr als 19.000 Mitarbeitern, 4,2 Milliarden Euro Umsatz und Standorten in über 60 Ländern der Welt eines der großen Familienunternehmen Europas.

Beratendes Gremium und Aufsichtsgremium sind der Gesellschafterausschuss beziehungsweise der Aufsichtsrat. Letzterer ist zugleich die kontrollierende Instanz gegenüber der Geschäftsführung.
 
Das operative Geschäft ist gebündelt in vier Konzernbereichen: Voith Digital Ventures, Voith Hydro, Voith Paper und Voith Turbo. Die Geschäfte der Tochtergesellschaften der Konzernbereiche werden jeweils über eine rechtlich selbstständige Führungsgesellschaft gesteuert.
description:
Job Description

About the Voith Group

The Voith Group is a global technology company. With its broad portfolio of systems, products, services and digital applications, Voith sets standards in the markets of energy, paper, raw materials and transport & automotive. Founded in 1867, the company today has around 22,000 employees, sales of € 5,5 billion and locations in over 60 countries worldwide and is thus one of the larger family-owned companies in Europe.

Support the Voith Group | Voith Hydro as a

Contract Administrator (m|f|d)
Job ID 73999 I Location Snowy/Australia

Ready for a new challenge (in down under)? Take your chance to be part of a great project in Australia!

Join one of the top global players in hydropower on one of the largest infrastructure projects in Australia!

Your tasks

Oversee all subcontract agreements, ensuring they're administered meticulously according to stipulations and provisions. This includes handling key subcontracts administration tasks such as correspondence, variations, claims, and payments.
Understand the head contract to properly manage subcontracts and protect the project's financial interests.
Collaborate with the procurement team to create and manage supplier and subcontractor tender packages. Analyse and negotiate offers to achieve cost-effective management.
Prepare subcontractor agreements, and coordinate reports. Ensure rigorous compliance with contractual obligations and verify that subcontractors and suppliers fulfill their commitments.
Maintain systematic records and archives in accordance with project procedures. Participate in cash flow forecasts for assigned contracts.
Process progress claims, monitor payment receipts and manage subcontractor claims, including time extensions and variations. Refer disputed claims to the subcontractor manager for decision.
Collaborate with team members to assess subcontractors' progress, performance and cost, and gather data for analysis and reporting.
Ensure all industrial requirements for each subcontractor on the project are approved and maintained. Maintain registers of contracts, variations, delays, extensions of time etc. for inclusion in reports.

Your profile

Bachelors Degree in Finance, Administration, Engineering, Quantity Surveying, Law or other relevant field.
At least 10 years of experience in contract administration.
Sound business acumen and solid background in tendering, financial management and contract administration and interpretation.
Experienced in claims and dispute resolution including leading negotiations.
Proven leadership, negotiating and lateral thinking skills.
Financial management – demonstrable behaviour in reducing costs and meeting budgets.
Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal.
Computer proficient, SAP experience is an advantage.

We offer you an international working environment with an open and inclusive company culture. In addition, you can expect flexible working models, sustainable development opportunities and various offers for the compatibility of family and career as well as health and well-being. Job Description

About the Voith Group

The Voith Group is a global technology company. With its broad portfolio of systems, products, services and digital applications, Voith sets standards in the markets of energy, paper, raw materials and transport & automotive. Founded in 1867, the company today has around 22,000 employees, sales of € 5,5 billion and locations in over 60 countries worldwide and is thus one of the larger family-owned companies in Europe.



Support the Voith Group | Voith Hydro as a



Contract Administrator (m|f|d)
Job ID 73999 I Location Snowy/Australia


Ready for a new challenge (in down under)? Take your chance to be part of a great project in Australia!

Join one of the top global players in hydropower on one of the largest infrastructure projects in Australia!


Your tasks

  • Oversee all subcontract agreements, ensuring they're administered meticulously according to stipulations and provisions. This includes handling key subcontracts administration tasks such as correspondence, variations, claims, and payments.
  • Understand the head contract to properly manage subcontracts and protect the project's financial interests.
  • Collaborate with the procurement team to create and manage supplier and subcontractor tender packages. Analyse and negotiate offers to achieve cost-effective management.
  • Prepare subcontractor agreements, and coordinate reports. Ensure rigorous compliance with contractual obligations and verify that subcontractors and suppliers fulfill their commitments.
  • Maintain systematic records and archives in accordance with project procedures. Participate in cash flow forecasts for assigned contracts.
  • Process progress claims, monitor payment receipts and manage subcontractor claims, including time extensions and variations. Refer disputed claims to the subcontractor manager for decision.
  • Collaborate with team members to assess subcontractors' progress, performance and cost, and gather data for analysis and reporting.
  • Ensure all industrial requirements for each subcontractor on the project are approved and maintained. Maintain registers of contracts, variations, delays, extensions of time etc. for inclusion in reports.

Your profile

  • Bachelors Degree in Finance, Administration, Engineering, Quantity Surveying, Law or other relevant field.
  • At least 10 years of experience in contract administration.
  • Sound business acumen and solid background in tendering, financial management and contract administration and interpretation.
  • Experienced in claims and dispute resolution including leading negotiations.
  • Proven leadership, negotiating and lateral thinking skills.
  • Financial management – demonstrable behaviour in reducing costs and meeting budgets.
  • Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal.
  • Computer proficient, SAP experience is an advantage.

We offer you an international working environment with an open and inclusive company culture. In addition, you can expect flexible working models, sustainable development opportunities and various offers for the compatibility of family and career as well as health and well-being. companyInfo/companyDescription:
Mensch, Stahl und Technologie – das sind wir. Bei uns steht der Mensch im Mittelpunkt: Denn erst durch unsere Mitarbeiterinnen und Mitarbeiter eröffnet sich der Blick auf die Vielfalt unserer Produkte und Dienstleistungen. Ob Spezialstähle für den automobilen Leichtbau, Rohre für Kraftwerke und Pipelines, Getränkeabfüllanlagen oder Spritzgießmaschinen – unser Spektrum ist so breit wie spannend.

Bei uns trifft Tradition auf Innovation und Pioniergeist. Wir schaffen ein Umfeld, in dem Sie Ihre Fähigkeiten voll entfalten und kontinuierlich weiterentwickeln können. Wir fördern Eigenverantwortung und einen offenen Austausch, so entstehen die besten Ideen und Lösungen.
Anerkennung, faire und leistungsorientierte Vergütung sowie umfangreiche Qualifizierungsangebote sind für uns selbstverständlich. Wir glauben an gemeinsamen Erfolg: Jeder Beitrag zählt, denn unser Erfolg ist immer der Erfolg jedes Einzelnen.

Wir wissen, wer wir sind, und wir sind stolz auf unser Handeln. Vielfalt ist unsere Stärke – in Produkten, Menschen und Ideen. Mit unserem Leitbild „YOUNITED – Gemeinsam mehr erreichen“ leben wir unsere Werte jeden Tag.

Sei Teil von etwas Großem – gestalte mit uns die Zukunft, in der Menschen, Technik und Visionen zusammenkommen.
description:
Process Engineer Utility Systems (m/f/d)

KHS is a subsidiary of Salzgitter AG. As one of the world's leading manufacturers of filling and packaging systems for beverages and liquid food we are a world-class player. Our customers have trusted in our passionate pioneering spirit and first-class technologies for over 150 years. However, we can only remain world class if we continue to find new employees who make just as high demands of themselves and the quality of their work as our customers make of us at KHS. Are you one of them?

We are currently seeking an Process Engineer Utility Systems (m/f/d) to join our “Innoplant” department at our Bad Kreuznach site.

Ihre Aufgaben
Intensive customer consulting and design of utility systems for turnkey projects in the beverage industry, worldwide.
Analysis of customer requirements and local conditions within the respective region.
Conceptual preparation of internal and external tender documents.
Planning of utility systems and media distribution for turnkey projects to ensure a reliable supply of all required media to production and filling lines.
Quotation preparation and project handling for utility systems in the beverage industry.
Supplier selection and technical clarification for utility systems as well as energy and environmental topics.
Project execution for utility systems in close cooperation with the Project Manager.
Ihr Profil
Technical training or degree (e.g., process engineering, mechanical engineering, or similar)
Several years of experience in technical sales or in the planning of industrial engineering systems, ideally in the food and beverage industry
Practical knowledge of industrial engineering systems in the food and beverage industry is desirable
Excellent knowledge of MS Office and MS Project
Business-fluent English; knowledge of another foreign language is a plus
Goal-oriented, structured approach to work, as well as the ability to work in a team, assertiveness, and a professional demeanor
Efficient time management, flexibility to work according to project requirements, strong customer focus, and willingness to travel internationally
Einsatzort: Bad Kreuznach ǀ Rheinland-Pfalz

companyInfo/companyDescription:
Die OHB System AG gehört zu den Top 3 Unternehmen der europäischen Raumfahrt und ist im börsennotierten Technologiekonzern OHB SE zu Hause.

Rund 2.800 Beschäftigte arbeiten in Deutschland, sowie weiteren EU-Ländern an bedeutenden Luft- und Raumfahrtprogrammen unserer Zeit. Dazu zählen erdnahe und geostationäre Satelliten für Erdbeobachtung, Navigation, Telekommunikation, Wissenschaft und Exploration des Weltraums. An den zwei starken Standorten der OHB System AG in Bremen und Oberpfaffenhofen entstehen die Satelliten ebenso wie Systeme für die Bemannte Raumfahrt, Luftaufklärung und Prozessleittechnik. Unseren Erfolg verdanken wir unseren Mitarbeitern und ihrer Leidenschaft für Innovation, ihrem Engagement und ihrer Initiative.
description:
We - MT Aerospace - are part of the OHB Space and Technology Group. As a leading international aerospace company, we develop, manufacture and test essential components for launcher programs such as Ariane, the U.S. Space Launch System (SLS) as well as commercial launcher systems, the Airbus fleet, spacecrafts, satellites and defense applications. Thanks to globally unique manufacturing technologies, we create high-performance products made of metal and fiber composites, such as large load-bearing structures and cryotanks, lightweight structures and fuel as well as pressure tanks that combine maximum performance with minimum weight. Especially our business areas of additive manufacturing, CFRP and hydrogen mobility make us a sought-after partner outside the aerospace sector as well. We currently employ around 600 people at our headquarter in Augsburg and at the launch site in Kourou (French Guiana).

Space. Far away for most - soon very close for you. Become part of an industry of visionaries and pioneers and make a valuable contribution as a Technical and administrative team support (m/f/d) to help the European launcher Ariane and other international launchers take off.

Both full-time (35 hours) and part-time (at least 28 hours) options are available.

Your Tasks

Providing administrative support to line managers and specialist managers, as well as project leaders, within the OZD and IOA departments
Assisting with departmental organisation, including asset management
Maintaining the OZD intranet section and drives in accordance with departmental guidelines
Editorial support in the preparation of specifications and tender documents
Support with external correspondence (e.g. with authorities and customs)
Preparation of purchase requisitions and call-off orders
Assistance with the management and maintenance of the OZD service portals
Support in the review of expense reports and the approval of invoices
Implementation of the staff onboarding and offboarding process, as well as coordination of internal relocations
Acting as a representative for maintaining the global room register, creating and amending technical workstations
Support with the coordination of the building cleaning service, including cost-plus contracts and monthly invoicing

Your Qualifications

Completed technical or commercial apprenticeship (or equivalent)
Experience as an assistant
Very good knowledge of MS Office
Solid knowledge of SAP desirable
Basic knowledge of project management is desirable
Experience in the manufacturing sector or industry is an advantage
Ability to work in a structured and independent manner
Ability to work as part of a team
We also welcome applicants returning to work after parental leave

We offer
Mobile working (according to the area of responsibility)
Flexible working hours and flexitime account
Attractive payment according to IG Metall collective agreement
35-hour week, 30 days annual leave and special leave days
Subject-specific training courses
In-house canteen
Employee parking spaces
Employee recommendation system
Benefits of a global group of companies combined with a familiar working atmosphere

Become part of our international team and take your professional and personal development to a new level. We are looking forward to your application (german or english) via our online tool.

If you have any questions, please contact career@mt-aerospace.de.

MT Aerospace AG
Human Resources

description:
Job Description

Are you an experienced Synchronous Machine and/ or power plant expert? Are you excited about participating in some of the largest infrastructure projects in Europe? Do you want to contribute actively to the green transition?

If this sounds like you, or you’re curious to learn more, then this role could be the perfect opportunity. Join our Power Systems Department as our new Powerplants specialist and work with us to close the gap to a sustainable future.

Your new role

As our new Powerplants and Generators Specialist, you will work alongside a dedicated team of Power Systems engineers across a broad range of projects, in Denmark and abroad. Your role will primarily involve tendering and detail design of power plants, including turbines, generators, and transformer systems, across a range of power plant types and sizes, including thermal powerplants.

Your key responsibilities will be:

Performing detailed engineering design of synchronous condensers and synchronous machines, including excitation systems, protection, and auxiliary systems.
Developing interface definitions and specifications between synchronous machines and the grid, including transformers, switchgear, protection and control, and grid compliance requirements.
Creating tender specifications and conducting technical evaluations for synchronous condensers, generators, excitation systems, and associated transformers.
Analysing reactive power, short-circuit contribution, inertia, and voltage control performance, and recommending system and control improvements.
Coordinating closely with civil, electrical, control, and grid-connection colleagues to ensure robust machine–grid and plant-level interfaces.
Ensuring strict compliance with applicable grid codes, HSE, and environmental regulations.

About you

Proven experience with design of power plants, power generating systems, and synchronous condensers.
Experience working with HV projects (between 132, 150, and 400 kV systems).
Experience with both greenfield and brownfield projects.
Consulting experience on large-scale power projects with a strong client-facing emphasis.
Experience with gas-fired power plants is desirable.
Fluent English and an additional European language.

What we can offer you

An outstanding opportunity to join the Ramboll Energy division consisting of more than 2000 experts globally which include approx. 200 Electrical Engineers.
Ongoing and committed investment in your development
Present leaders guided by our Leadership Principles
A culture that welcomes you as the unique person you are
The long-term thinking of a foundation-owned company

Work at the heart of sustainable change

Ramboll is a global architecture, engineering, and consulting firm. We believe that the goal of sustainable change is to create a thriving world for both nature and people. That's where we begin—and how we work. At Ramboll, our people are our core strength, and our history is rooted in a clear vision of how a responsible company should behave. Openness and curiosity are the cornerstones of our culture. We embrace an inclusive mindset that seeks fresh, diverse, and innovative perspectives. We respect, embrace, and invite diversity in all its forms to actively create an environment where everyone can thrive and reach their full potential.

Ready to join us?

Please submit your application. Be sure to include all relevant documents including your CV and cover letter.

Thank you for your time and we look forward to receiving your application.

About Ramboll

Founded in Denmark, Ramboll is a foundation-owned people company. We have more than 18,000 experts working across our global operations in 35 countries. Our experts are leaders in their fields, developing and delivering innovative solutions in diverse markets including Buildings, Transport, Planning & Urban Design, Water, Environment & Health, Energy, and Management Consulting. We invite you to contribute to a more sustainable future working in an open, collaborative, and empowering company. Combining local experience with global knowledge, we together shape the societies of tomorrow.

Equality, diversity, and inclusion is at the heart of what we do

We believe in the strength of diversity and know that unique experiences and perspectives are vital for creating truly sustainable societies. Therefore, we are committed to providing an inclusive and supportive work environment where everyone can flourish and reach their potential. We welcome applications from candidates of all backgrounds and encourage you to contact our recruitment team to discuss any accommodations you need during the application process

description:
Job Description

Are you ready to support innovation in Cost Estimation while promoting sustainable energy solutions on a global scale? Can you contribute to a growing team with the goal of enhancing professionalism within the Cost Estimation process? Do you thrive off ensuring quality and working in an international environment?

If this sounds like you, then this role could be the perfect fit. Join our team as our new Principal Cost Estimator and help us close the gap to a sustainable future.

Your new Team

As our new Principal Cost Estimator, you will play a pivotal role in our Global Cost Estimation Competence Centre.

You’ll work alongside high-performing specialists to provide robust cost estimation support across the full project lifecycle - from tender and proposal stage through to monitoring and analysing costs at various stages of project delivery - ensuring our position as a trusted partner to clients worldwide.

The Cost Estimation department is characterized by an informal and humorous collaboration, where we bring the individual strengths into play through close collaboration. The department is a Global Competence Centre and colleagues are therefore located in several locations.

Your key tasks and responsibilities will be:

Lifecycle Estimating:
Cost Estimation based upon AACE methodology (Class 1-5) across the full project lifecycle, from feasibility/FID to execution/delivery.
Perform cost reconciliation and lessons learned during project close-out.
Evaluate cost implications of green technologies and PtX solutions to support decarbonization goals.
Advise on CAPEX/OPEX trade-offs for sustainable design.
Risk & Contingency Analysis:
Conduct quantitative risk analysis and develop contingency strategies aligned with AACE standards.
Support probabilistic estimating and scenario planning.
Cost Control Integration:
Collaborate with project controls for cost tracking, forecasting, and earned value management.
Assess cost impacts of scope changes and variations during execution.
Standards, Methodology, Digital Tools & Innovation:
Drive automation and data analytics initiatives to enhance accuracy and efficiency.
Continuously update cost databases, guidelines, and procedures.
Apply AACE International Recommended Practices
Stakeholder Engagement:
Working collaboratively with project managers and discipline leads and procurement teams.
Present estimates and assumptions clearly to senior management and clients.
Lead and participate in workshops to identify cost-saving alternatives without compromising functionality.

About you

We will support your personal and professional development from day one. For this role, the following qualifications and skills will set you up for success:

Minimum 8 years of experience with cost estimation
Minimum Bachelor’s degree in an Engineering or related discipline.
Preference for AACE CCP (Certified Cost Professional) or equivalent certification.
Significant and measurable experience with cost estimation in the industrial or energy transition (green energy) sectors is essential, ideally within renewable projects such as hydrogen, CCS, chemical process industry and decarbonization projects. Candidates from closely related industries such as Offshore Oil & Gas will also be considered.
Strong commercials skills in the areas of project finance and contractual matters.
Support business development activities towards new and existing clients
Experience with estimating software and database systems; ability to contribute to method development and digitalization.
Good English skills – both verbal and written
Right to work in the advertised location.

Personal qualities that will help you succeed in this role include proactivity, drive, client focus, collaborative, and ability to influence decisions effectively.

What we can offer you

Investment in your development
Leaders you can count on, guided by our Leadership Principles
Be valued for the unique person you are
Never be short of inspiration from colleagues, clients, and projects
The long-term thinking of a foundation-owned company

Work at the heart of sustainable change

Ramboll is a global architecture, engineering, and consultancy company. We believe that the purpose of sustainable change is to create a thriving world for both nature and people. So, that’s where we start – and how we work. At Ramboll, our core strength is our people, and our history is rooted in a clear vision of how a responsible company should act. Being open and curious is a cornerstone of our culture. We embrace an inclusive mindset that looks for fresh, diverse, and innovative perspectives. We respect, embrace, and invite diversity in all forms to actively cultivate an environment where everyone can flourish and realise their full potential.

About Ramboll

Founded in Denmark, Ramboll is a foundation-owned people company. We have more than 18,000 experts working across our global operations in 35 countries. Our experts are leaders in their fields, developing and delivering innovative solutions in diverse markets including Buildings, Transport, Planning & Urban Design, Water, Environment & Health, Energy, and Management Consulting. We invite you to contribute to a more sustainable future working in an open, collaborative, and empowering company. Combining local experience with global knowledge, we together shape the societies of tomorrow.

We believe in the strength of diversity and know that unique experiences and perspectives are vital for creating truly sustainable societies. Personal qualities that will help you succeed in this role include proactivity, drive, client focus, collaborative, and ability to influence decisions effectively.


What we can offer you

  • Investment in your development
  • Leaders you can count on, guided by our Leadership Principles
  • Be valued for the unique person you are
  • Never be short of inspiration from colleagues, clients, and projects
  • The long-term thinking of a foundation-owned company


Work at the heart of sustainable change

Ramboll is a global architecture, engineering, and consultancy company. We believe that the purpose of sustainable change is to create a thriving world for both nature and people. So, that’s where we start – and how we work. At Ramboll, our core strength is our people, and our history is rooted in a clear vision of how a responsible company should act. Being open and curious is a cornerstone of our culture. We embrace an inclusive mindset that looks for fresh, diverse, and innovative perspectives. We respect, embrace, and invite diversity in all forms to actively cultivate an environment where everyone can flourish and realise their full potential.


About Ramboll

Founded in Denmark, Ramboll is a foundation-owned people company. We have more than 18,000 experts working across our global operations in 35 countries. Our experts are leaders in their fields, developing and delivering innovative solutions in diverse markets including Buildings, Transport, Planning & Urban Design, Water, Environment & Health, Energy, and Management Consulting. We invite you to contribute to a more sustainable future working in an open, collaborative, and empowering company. Combining local experience with global knowledge, we together shape the societies of tomorrow.


Equality, diversity, and inclusion is at the heart of what we do

We believe in the strength of diversity and know that unique experiences and perspectives are vital for creating truly sustainable societies. Therefore, we are committed to providing an inclusive and supportive work environment where everyone can flourish and reach their potential. We welcome applications from candidates of all backgrounds and encourage you to contact our recruitment team to discuss any accommodations you need during the application process.


Additional Information


All your information will be kept confidential according to EEO guidelines.

companyInfo/companyDescription:
Die OHB System AG gehört zu den Top 3 Unternehmen der europäischen Raumfahrt und ist im börsennotierten Technologiekonzern OHB SE zu Hause.

Rund 2.800 Beschäftigte arbeiten in Deutschland, sowie weiteren EU-Ländern an bedeutenden Luft- und Raumfahrtprogrammen unserer Zeit. Dazu zählen erdnahe und geostationäre Satelliten für Erdbeobachtung, Navigation, Telekommunikation, Wissenschaft und Exploration des Weltraums. An den zwei starken Standorten der OHB System AG in Bremen und Oberpfaffenhofen entstehen die Satelliten ebenso wie Systeme für die Bemannte Raumfahrt, Luftaufklärung und Prozessleittechnik. Unseren Erfolg verdanken wir unseren Mitarbeitern und ihrer Leidenschaft für Innovation, ihrem Engagement und ihrer Initiative.
description:
Die OHB Digital Connect GmbH ist ein Tochterunternehmen des in Bremen ansässigen Technologie- und Raumfahrtkonzerns OHB SE. Im Fokus ihrer geschäftlichen Aktivitäten stehen bodengestützte Systeme und Services. Dazu zählen in erster Linie der Betrieb von Satelliten, sowie die Bereitstellung von IT-Systemen und Applikationen zur Nutzung satellitengenerierter Daten. Durch die Kombination von verschiedenen Sensortechnologien und Datenlinks können darüber hinaus im Bereich Erdbeobachtung und Aufklärung umfassende Lösungen zur Gewinnung und sicheren Verarbeitung von Daten angeboten werden. Als erfahrener Anbieter von Satellitenbodensystemen, Antennen und Teleskopen ist die OHB Digital Connect GmbH zudem ein zuverlässiger Partner für die Entwicklung mechatronischer Systeme. Mit diesem breit aufgestellten Portfolio kann die OHB Digital Connect GmbH den Anforderungen von Kunden aus den unterschiedlichsten Branchen und Marktsegmenten gerecht werden.

Die OHB Digital Connect GmbH sucht am Standort Bremen für eine unbefristete Festeinstellung eine:n

Subcontractor Manager:in (m/w/d)

Ihre Aufgaben

Als verantwortliche:r Lieferantenmanager:in besteht die Hauptaufgabe darin, die Versorgung der Satellitenkommunikations-Bodensegmente mit komplexen Zuliefergeräten und Subsystemen sicherzustellen. Dies beinhaltet im Einzelnen folgende Arbeitsinhalte:

Mitwirkung an der Vertragsgestaltung und aktives Führen von Verhandlungen
Führen des Lieferanten und Managen der wesentlichen Reviews beim Lieferanten während der Vertragslaufzeit
Überwachung von Zulieferern u.a. durch Sicherstellung der Einhaltung vorgegebener Kostenrahmen, Qualitätsstandards, Arbeitsanweisungen und Terminvorgaben
Bewertung der Lieferantenperformance und Einleiten erforderlicher Maßnahmen sowie deren Nachverfolgung
Koordination und Führung von Auswahlgremien geeigneter Lieferanten unter Berücksichtigung projektspezifischer Anforderungen und historischer Performance
Koordination und Verhandlung von Vertragsänderungen
Unterstützung des Projektmanagements beim Risikomanagement insbesondere der Risikominimierung in Bezug auf den Lieferanten
Enge Zusammenarbeit mit der Qualitätsabteilung zur Sicherstellung und Einhaltung geltender Qualitätsstandards
Gestaltung und Unterstützung bei Ausschreibungen und Angebotsaufforderungen u.a. Leitung von Lieferantenauswahlprozessen (RFP, Evaluation, Verhandlung, Tender Evaluation)

Ihre Qualifikationen

Bevorzugt abgeschlossenes Ingenieursstudium, ggf. Wirtschaftsingenieursstudium oder Studium der Wirtschaftswissenschaften
Mehrjährige Berufserfahrung in dem Bereich „Procurement Management“ / „Subcontractor Management“ / „Supply Chain Management“, idealerweise im Bereich der Satellitenkommunikation und/oder Bodensegmenten
Erfahrung in Verhandlungsführung und Projektmanagement
Eine hohe technische Affinität
Hohe Motivation, Engagement und Verantwortungsbewusstsein
Ausgeprägte Kommunikationsstärke
Fähigkeit zur Entscheidungsfindung und Konfliktlösung
Sicheres Auftreten gegenüber Kunden, Lieferanten und Unterauftragnehmern
Exzellente konzeptuelle und analytische Fähigkeiten
Sehr gute deutsch und englische Sprachkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift
Erfahrung in der fachlichen Führung von Teams und komplexen Projekten
Strategisches Denken und ein gutes Verständnis des Marktes sowie der Kundenanforderungen
Reisebereitschaft

Wir bieten
Work Life Balance: flexible Arbeitszeiten, Sabbatical, 30 Urlaubstage & Sonderurlaubstage
Mobiles Arbeiten: hybrides Arbeitsmodell & mobiles Arbeiten im Ausland
Strukturierter Onboarding-Prozess (inkl. Begleitung durch einen Buddy)
Entwicklung: Umfangreiches Trainingsangebot, individuelle Weiterentwicklung & jährlicher Mitarbeiterdialog
Raumfahrtprojekte
Zusatzleistungen: 13. Bruttomonatsgehalt, Unfallversicherung & betriebliche Altersvorsorge
Verpflegung: Hochwertiges Betriebsrestaurant & großzügiger Essenszuschuss
Aktive Gesundheitsförderung: Gesundheitsmanagement, E-GYM Wellpass & Sportgruppen
Familie: Kindergartenzuschuss / Kindergartenplätze, Kinderferienbetreuung & bezahlte Kindkrank-Tage
Mobilität: Bezuschusstes Deutschlandticket, Jobrad & Parkplätze
Firmenfeiern & Events: Weihnachtsfeier, Satellite Launch Events, NextGen Speakers Night
Innovation: Betriebliches Vorschlagswesen & Freie Forschung und Entwicklung

Mehr als 150 Jahre inspirierende Technologie für Generationen

Voith ist ein weltweit agierender Technologiekonzern. Mit seinem breiten Portfolio aus Anlagen, Produkten, Serviceleistungen und digitalen Anwendungen setzt Voith Maßstäbe in den Märkten Energie, Öl & Gas, Papier, Rohstoffe und Transport & Automotive. Gegründet 1867 ist Voith heute mit mehr als 19.000 Mitarbeitern, 4,2 Milliarden Euro Umsatz und Standorten in über 60 Ländern der Welt eines der großen Familienunternehmen Europas.

Beratendes Gremium und Aufsichtsgremium sind der Gesellschafterausschuss beziehungsweise der Aufsichtsrat. Letzterer ist zugleich die kontrollierende Instanz gegenüber der Geschäftsführung.
 
Das operative Geschäft ist gebündelt in vier Konzernbereichen: Voith Digital Ventures, Voith Hydro, Voith Paper und Voith Turbo. Die Geschäfte der Tochtergesellschaften der Konzernbereiche werden jeweils über eine rechtlich selbstständige Führungsgesellschaft gesteuert.
description:
Job Description

About the Voith Group

The Voith Group is a global technology company. With its broad portfolio of systems, products, services and digital applications, Voith sets standards in the markets of energy, paper, raw materials and transport & automotive. Founded in 1867, the company today has around 20,700 employees, sales of € 4,8 billion and locations in over 60 countries worldwide and is thus one of the larger family-owned companies in Europe.

Support the Voith Group | Group Division Hydro as a

Tender Lead Project Planner (m/f/d)
Job ID 78099 | Location world wide

Your Tasks

Develop the end-to-end overall schedule during the offer phase (OTO) for new-build and modernization projects by integrating inputs from all relevant functions, units, and third parties.
Define the underlying planning logic of the project time schedule, including whether the schedule structure should be primarily driven by engineering or site activities.
Collect, clarify, and consolidate schedule-relevant inputs such as project framework conditions, key dates, scope of supply, engineering sequences, sourcing scenarios, logistics assumptions, installation concepts, and commissioning requirements.
Coordinate closely with OPM, Engineering, Procurement, Manufacturing, Transport, Installation, Commissioning, and other relevant stakeholders to establish a consistent and feasible overall schedule.
Clarify technical dependencies, durations, and sequencing for System Engineering and Component Engineering activities in coordination with the relevant engineering functions and integrate them into the overall schedule.
Incorporate contractual customer approval processes, milestone structures, and schedule-related contractual requirements into the overall schedule.
Assess sourcing strategies, countries of origin, procurement / manufacturing durations, transport concepts, and pre-assembly / factory assembly assumptions from a scheduling perspective.
Ensure that all relevant interfaces with customers, consortium partners, other OUs, sub-suppliers, and external parties are reflected appropriately in the overall schedule.
Perform schedule analyses, including critical path, multiple critical paths, and near-critical paths, in order to identify key risks and opportunities during the offer phase.
Prepare alternative overall schedules, scenarios, and planning options to support offer strategy, feasibility assessment, and internal decision-making.
Review schedule quality in line with the DCMA 14 Point Assessment criteria.
Support and coordinate internal schedule alignment and review processes, including participation in Design Freeze 0 (DF0) and preparation / coordination of the OTO SFAC (Schedule Freeze Acceptance Certificate).
Update and revise the overall schedule during the negotiation phase in the event of customer changes or revised project assumptions.
Ensure a structured handover of the overall schedule from OTO to OTC once the order is received.

Your Profile

Degree in engineering, project management, industrial engineering, or a comparable field.
Several years of professional experience in project planning, scheduling, or project controls, ideally in complex technical project environments.
Solid experience in developing and coordinating overall schedules in multidisciplinary project environments.
Strong understanding of planning and schedule control principles, including WBS, critical path methodology, float logic, progress measurement, and interface management.
Experience with planning tools such as Primavera P6 and/or MS Project.
Ability to collect, structure, assess, and consolidate inputs from multiple specialist functions into one coherent planning logic.
Good understanding of engineering, procurement / manufacturing, transport, installation, and commissioning interfaces within complex project schedules.
Strong coordination and communication skills, with the ability to work effectively across functions and organizational interfaces.
Analytical thinking, a structured working style, sound judgment, and the ability to challenge assumptions and identify inconsistencies in available data.
Proactive, reliable, and solution-oriented approach, with the ability to support decision-making in a transparent and fact-based manner.
Experience in hydro, energy, plant engineering, or other large-scale industrial project environments is an advantage.

This position can be filled at any of our global Voith Hydro locations (accept Germany). The remote work policies will adhere to the regulations of the specific location where you are based - this is not a fully remote position.

We offer you an international working environment with an open and inclusive company culture. In addition, you can expect flexible working models, sustainable development opportunities and various offers for the compatibility of family and career as well as health and well-being.

 via our web portal.

Find more details about the application process.

The J.M. Voith SE & Co. KG | Voith Hydro is looking forward to your application!

Your personal contact
J.M. Voith SE & Co. KG | VZS
Global Business Services HR EMEA
Talent Acquisition Team • Anna Rau • phone +49 7321 37 9587

With the submission of your application, your personal data will be processed and transferred within the HR departments of our company group – and, if necessary, also worldwide – for the purpose of conducting the recruitment process. This is carried out in compliance with the applicable data protection regulations. For more information on data protection and your rights, please refer to our privacy policy on our careers website.

companyInfo/companyDescription:
Giesecke+Devrient (G+D) is a global SecurityTech company headquartered in Munich, Germany. G+D makes the lives of billions of people more secure. The company shapes trust in the digital age, with built-in security technology in three segments: Digital Security, Financial Platforms and Currency Technology.

G+D was founded in 1852 and today has a workforce of more than 14,000 employees. In the fiscal year 2023, the company generated a turnover of 3 billion euros. G+D is represented by 123 subsidiaries and joint ventures in 40 countries. Further information: www.gi-de.com.
description:
G+D makes the lives of billions of people around the world more secure. We create trust in the digital age with integrated security technologies in three business areas: Digital Security, Financial Platforms and Currency Technology. We have been a reliable partner for our customers for over 170 years with our innovative solutions for SecurityTech! We are an international technology group and traditional family business with over 14,000 employees in 40 countries. Creating Confidence is our path to success. Trust is the basis of our co-operation within G+D.

The whole world trusts us when it comes to physical or digital currencies. We increase the security and efficiency of the cash cycle in collaboration with central banks and the entire currency industry. As the market leader in advanced currency management, would you like to join us in shaping the future of payments?

Your Tasks:
You take on bid management with a focus on project calculations and financial evaluations of tenders and proposals – particularly in the areas of cash center solutions and high-security printing.
You provide cross-functional support to the Bid and Contract Management team EU/LATAM/NAM.
You work closely and as an equal partner with our Sales, Technical Sales teams, the Contract Managers in Munich, as well as with our international subsidiaries.
You analyze customer-specific tender documents and develop a coherent bid strategy together with the international bid team.
You clarify project-related questions with the relevant departments – such as Export, Logistics, Insurance, Compliance, Legal, Procurement, Controlling, Tax, or Finance.
You prepare consolidated project costings, conduct risk assessments, and manage the internal approval process in line with company guidelines.
You identify opportunities, risks, and optimization potential within cost structures and derive concrete recommendations for action.
When required, you support the Sales team in commercial contract negotiations with customers.
You ensure accurate and audit-proof documentation and compliance with all formal and commercial requirements.
Once a contract is signed, you are responsible for the structured handover to the operational teams.
Your Profile:
You have completed a commercial apprenticeship and/or hold a university degree in Business Administration or STEM.
You have several years of professional experience in bid management, ideally combined with (sales) controlling or accounting.
You bring expert-level knowledge in cost and pricing calculations as well as in presenting commercial KPIs and financial metrics.
Risk assessments, scenario calculations, and cost structure analyses are among your strengths and motivate you.
You are capable of analyzing complex specifications and tender documents and translating them into accurate calculation models.
You have experience working with international standards and public procurement processes.
You possess basic contract knowledge, including in software-related environments.
Flexibility, analytical thinking, initiative, and a solution-oriented, structured working style distinguish you – complemented by a healthy degree of pragmatism.
You are fluent in German and English; an additional foreign language is an advantage.
You are willing to occasionally travel internationally to support customer negotiations together with Sales and/or Technical teams.
You are proficient in MS Office, especially Excel (including pivot tables).

Contact
Hannah Distler
career@gi-de.com

  • You identify opportunities, risks, and optimization potential within cost structures and derive concrete recommendations for action.
  • When required, you support the Sales team in commercial contract negotiations with customers.
  • You ensure accurate and audit-proof documentation and compliance with all formal and commercial requirements.
  • Once a contract is signed, you are responsible for the structured handover to the operational teams.

Your Profile:

  • You have completed a commercial apprenticeship and/or hold a university degree in Business Administration or STEM.
  • You have several years of professional experience in bid management, ideally combined with (sales) controlling or accounting.
  • You bring expert-level knowledge in cost and pricing calculations as well as in presenting commercial KPIs and financial metrics.
  • Risk assessments, scenario calculations, and cost structure analyses are among your strengths and motivate you.
  • You are capable of analyzing complex specifications and tender documents and translating them into accurate calculation models.
  • You have experience working with international standards and public procurement processes.
  • You possess basic contract knowledge, including in software-related environments.
  • Flexibility, analytical thinking, initiative, and a solution-oriented, structured working style distinguish you – complemented by a healthy degree of pragmatism.
  • You are fluent in German and English; an additional foreign language is an advantage.
  • You are willing to occasionally travel internationally to support customer negotiations together with Sales and/or Technical teams.
  • You are proficient in MS Office, especially Excel (including pivot tables).


Contact

Hannah Distler

companyInfo/companyDescription:
Visionen. Wege. Weitblick.
Über den Tellerrand schauen, Initiative ergreifen und Chancen nutzen – Knauf zählt heute mit über 40.000 Mitarbeitern und einem Umsatz von rund 12,6 Mrd. € zu den führenden Herstellern von Baustoffen und Bausystemen in Europa und weit darüber hinaus. Mit über 300 Produktionsstätten sind wir heute auf allen Kontinenten vertreten und als Global Player erfolgreich in der Welt zuhause.

Unsere Vision – der Gipfel 
- Wir wollen in allen Teilen der Welt das Bauen einfacher und effizienter gestalten. 
- Mit unseren innovativen Baustoffen führen wir die Märkte an und wollen auch in der digitalen Welt 
  Marktführer sein. 
- Mit unternehmerischem Weitblick gehen wir offen aber bodenständig in die Zukunft.

Unsere Mission – der Weg 
- Wir setzen Maßstäbe bei Produkten und Systemen. 
- Unsere Baustoffe aus Gips sowie unsere Dämmstoffe sind innovativ, energieeffizient, preisgerecht und nachhaltig. 
- Wir erreichen stets überdurchschnittliches und profitables Wachstum. 
- Motivierte, engagierte und offene Mitarbeiter sind unser größtes Kapital. 
- Unsere Kunden stehen im Mittelpunkt all unserer Bemühungen.

Unsere Basis – familiäre Werte
Wir sind ein Familienunternehmen. Unser Denken und Handeln bei Knauf orientiert sich seit der Unternehmensgründung an klaren Werten: 
- Menschlichkeit
- Partnerschaft
- Engagement
- Unternehmergeist
description:
Knauf stands for opportunity. This exciting role offers you the ideal opportunity to begin and further develop an impressive career within a values-driven culture.

As a global building materials manufacturer with around 43,500 employees in over 90 countries, we offer diverse opportunities to ambitious talent. We value every contribution and are committed to creating a safe and inclusive work environment with the aim of developing sustainable solutions for a better future.

Gebr. Knauf KG is proud to be a part of the Knauf Group. With a long-standing tradition, and a growing international presence, we are seeking dedicated individuals to help us achieve our long-term vision.

Can you say ‘yes’?

Is respectful and appreciative interaction within the team a given for you?

Do you always put the customer at the center of your actions?

Do you consider the future and upcoming generations in your actions?

Do you strive for personal and professional growth?

What you'll do

Define the EMEA In- & Outbound Logistics Excellence strategy and roadmap aligned to global standards; execute regional change and communications plans; build a best‑practice community and champion network; define and drive continuous improvement initiatives

Lead end‑to‑end deployment of strategic regional systems (e.g., SAP TM, Transporeon) - from pre‑engagement planning and business readiness to rollout and post‑go‑live stabilization - ensuring no disruption to service

Establish a structured cadence of EMEA business reviews; leverage data‑driven insights to steer performance; identify gaps and deploy the Excellence team to stabilize operations and mitigate risks

Directly manage Excellence and Implementation managers; ensure teams provide local organizations with the tools, training, and process frameworks needed to meet and exceed SLAs

Monitor operational governance and SLA management; align logistics tenders with procurement to optimize supply chain and vendor performance across EMEA

Who you are

Extensive experience in senior roles within Logistics/ Supply Chain Operational Excellence, Process Management, or Transformation, with a strong track record of leading large-scale programs and improving performance

Proven experience managing large initiatives and cross-border deployments of ERP, TMS, and WMS - from design through go-live and stabilization

Expert at dissecting complex business reviews, interrogating operational metrics, and pinpointing root causes to drive effective corrective action

Skilled in aligning Procurement, Operations, Sales, and other teams around a single excellence standard; prepares organizations for “next-level” operations while maintaining BAU stability

University degree in Business, Supply Chain, or Engineering; Lean Six Sigma Black Belt preferred

What we offer you

We all shape our lives individually. As an employer, we play a significant role in the daily life of our employees and aim to support them with various offerings. This includes, among other things:

Security: Permanent employment contract in a stable, successful family-owned company.

Flexibility: 30 days of vacation, special leave for certain occasions, flexitime account with a broad time window for flexible working and weekly home office days.

Fitness and health: Discounts on various sports and leisure activities (e.g., fitness studio Iphofen, swimming training, soccer, etc.), as well as opportunities for active health promotion through our company doctor and occupational health and safety offers.

Daily time savings: Ample parking and direct access to public transport from the company premises (train, bus).

Future planning: Subsidies for capital-forming benefits and offers for company pension schemes.

Welcome approach: Individual onboarding days, company and team events, subsidized company canteen and our Barista coffee bar.

Career and professional development: Individual support and promotion for part-time studies, further education and training.

Benefits and vouchers: Discounts on well-known brands and reduced-price employee purchases.

E-Bike leasing: Using tax advantages while promoting your own health.

Relocation support: Assistance with international relocation

Knauf as an employer

Knauf is one of the world's leading manufacturers of modern insulation materials, dry construction systems, plasters and accessories, external thermal insulation composite systems, paints, screeds, flooring systems as well as construction equipment and tools. With more than 300 production sites and sales organizations in over 90 countries, the Knauf Group is undoubtedly one of the big players on the world market.

The Knauf Group is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to a diverse and inclusive work environment, and we make all personnel decisions across all areas of our company based on experience, skills and integrity. We encourage applicants from all walks of life to apply for our positions, regardless of age, gender, gender identity, disability, sexual orientation, origin, religion and so forth.

Share our values and become part of the team to shape the future of Knauf together.
